Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.
A garden rest area seems to offer the group a brief moment of respite.
Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.
The passage divides into two here, leaving you with no idea as to which direction to take.
The T-junction will always be empty, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event is rolled during the Power Phase.
A leafy tree fills the room, offering some shade all around it. Ideal for a brief pause.
Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.
In the centre of the plaza, an immense monument rises. It seems especially strange when the moonlight reflects on it.
Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.
Beneath the great grate, water runs, falls, and splashes everywhere. It might be an escape route.
Upon entering a Dungeon Room, draw an Event Card unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.
The city centre lies abandoned. As you arrive, you sense a kind of magical aura surrounding the place. Everything is in ruins after the siege.
For details about this room, consult the Adventure Book.
You see deep, murky water and are fairly certain that something just swam by.
See the Hall of the Hag Queen rulebook for details of the Water Cave.
A towering statue of Khaine greets visitors. On a platform behind it is a cauldron dripping with blood.
See the Hall of the Hag Queen rulebook for details about the Temple of Khaine. Choose one of two doorway positions.
Hovering over a bottomless pit is a series of wooden planks tied precariously together.
See the Hall of the Hag Queen rulebook for details of the Bottomless Pit.
Protruding out of the cave walls are enormous crystals which seem to pulsate with energy.
The first time a Warrior enters this Board Section they roll 1D6 and add that number to the Power Phase roll. The total is the number of Wounds they heal, up to their Starting Wounds score.
This shadowy corridor leads to a flight of steps.
The Landing will always be empty unless an Unexpected Event is rolled during the Power Phase. Choose one of two doorway positions.
You almost stumble and fall down a set of slimy stone steps that descend into the darkness.
The Stairway will always be empty unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event is rolled during the Power Phase.
The dim light of your lantern illuminates what appears to be a floor covered in a checkerboard pattern.
The Guard Room will always trigger an Event Card, unless the Adventure Book states otherwise.
A deep red light seeps out from this room, suffusing the area with a burning glow. The air is almost too hot to breathe and the walls are warm to the touch.
See the Adventure Book for details of the Firechasm.
The corridor suddenly and sharply twists into the darkness.
The Corner will always be empty unless the Adventure Book states otherwise or an Unexpected Event is rolled during the Power Phase.
A robed skeleton kneels before a stone lectern, its bony hands still clasped in prayer.

If the Warriors lay the Abbot's remains to rest, no Unexpected Event is rolled in the next Power Phase. In addition, hidden in the lectern is a single healing draught that restores 2D6 Wounds to one Warrior. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Massive paw prints and staff marks cross the dust and vanish into a crack in the wall.

The Warriors follow the signs of an Amber Wizard and learn something of the creatures ahead. The next time you roll on a Monster Table, you may reroll the result once or resolve it as if the party were one Battle-level lower. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A bronze death-mask of a Dwarf lord hangs from an iron peg, waiting for a worthy bearer.

One Warrior may wear the mask into the next combat. Until that combat ends he gains +1 Toughness and cannot be pinned by Undead. At the end of the combat roll 1D6. On a score of 1 the ancient spirit departs in anger and the Warrior suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Pale fungus bursts underfoot, filling the room with madly glittering spores.

Each Warrior must roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 he coughs and staggers, suffering 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. On a score of 3 or 4 he is merely dazed and is at -1 to hit until the end of the next Warriors' Phase. On a score of 5 or 6 he fights off the fumes.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
An Imperial battle-banner lies half buried in rubble, its faded colors still stubbornly bright beneath the grime.

One Warrior may carry the banner into the next combat. Until that combat ends he gains +1 Toughness and cannot be pinned. If the Warriors win that combat while the banner is still borne, each Warrior gains 50 gold from the confidence and discipline it inspires in their search. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A black cloud of cave bats explodes from the roof, filling the chamber with claws and wings.

Randomly determine which Warrior is engulfed by the swarm. He suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. In addition, all Warriors on this board section are at -1 to hit until the end of the next Warriors' Phase as they fight through the chaos.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A foul totem of horn and hide leers from the corner, surrounded by crude fetishes and old blood.

The Warriors may smash the totem or leave it be. If they smash it, each Warrior gains 1D6 x 10 gold in teeth, charms and trinkets, but the next Beastmen the party fights attack immediately. If they leave it untouched, the next time the party fights Beastmen each Warrior gains +1 to hit in the first round instead. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A cruelly beautiful amulet of Dark Elf make glitters amongst a scatter of old bones.

One Warrior may wear the trinket into the next combat. In the first round he gains +1 Attack and +1 to hit. However, if he rolls any natural 1 to hit during that combat, the amulet bites into his flesh and he suffers 1 Wound, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A stray spark ignites a forgotten cache of powder left behind by some ill-fated engineer.

There is a deafening blast. Every model on this board section suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. Loose stones, smoke and ringing ears fill the chamber for a few moments before the dust settles.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
Dark symbols ooze fresh blood as soon as the Warriors step across them.

Randomly determine which Warrior the runes demand tribute from. He suffers 1D6 + 1 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. The rune-fire then gutters out, leaving the stones slick and smoking.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
The ceiling cracks open and showers the room with splintered bones and jagged stone.

Each Warrior on this board section must roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 he suffers 1D6 Wounds from the razor-sharp fragments. Normal Toughness applies, but armour offers no protection against the hail of debris.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A faded grail-shield hangs above a cracked stone bench, as if waiting for one more oath to be sworn.

One Warrior may kneel and swear to face the darkness without flinching. In the first round of the next combat that Warrior gains +2 Attacks. If he is reduced to 0 Wounds in that combat, the blessing is considered spent whether he survives or not. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
The crackle of hidden pressure is followed by a roaring jet of sulphurous flame.

Roll 1D6 for each Warrior. The Warrior with the lowest score has triggered a vent in the floor and suffers 2D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. In addition, the choking smoke leaves him at -1 to hit until the end of the next Warriors' Phase.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A powder horn and engineer's charges are wrapped in oilskin beneath a slab near the wall.

The Warriors may save the charge for battle or for obstacles. If used in battle, one Warrior of the party's choice gains +2 Damage on the first successful hit he inflicts in the next combat. If used on the way ahead, the next blocked door or portcullis encountered is blown open automatically. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Neat chalk arrows and distances are written on the wall in the hand of a careful explorer.

The markings are recent enough to trust. The Warriors may look at the next four Dungeon cards and reorder them as they wish. If one of those cards is an objective room, it must remain within those four cards. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A corroded chain snaps and an iron cauldron overturns in a flood of scalding slime.

Roll 1D6 for each Warrior. The Warrior with the lowest score is drenched by the boiling muck and suffers 2D6 Wounds. Toughness applies normally, but armour offers no protection. He is also at -1 Move until the end of the next Warriors' Phase.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
Clouds of tiny stinging vermin pour from a cracked plague censer at the Warriors' feet.

Each Warrior must roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 he suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. On a score of 3 or 4 he is not badly hurt but is left weakened and is at -1 Toughness until the end of the next combat. On a score of 5 or 6 he brushes the creatures aside.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
Heavy chains rattle overhead and a spiked flail swings out of a hidden slot.

Roll 1D6 for each Warrior. The Warrior with the lowest score is smashed aside by the pendulum and suffers 2D6 Wounds, with no modifier for armour except a helmet. He is also pinned until the end of the next Warriors' Phase.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
Iron tablets etched in harsh runes record tribute, patrol routes and the names of slaves long dead.

If the Warriors take time to decipher the tablets, they may make the next roll on a Monster Table as if the party were one Battle-level lower. A Dwarf or Wizard may read the ledger automatically. Any other Warrior must roll 1D6 and score 5 or 6 to make use of it. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Pale witchfire races across the floor in a ring around the Warriors.

Each Warrior on this board section must roll 1D6. On a score of 1, 2 or 3 he is scorched by unnatural flames and suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. On a score of 4, 5 or 6 he leaps clear before the blue fire can bite.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A courier in Imperial livery lies where he fell, his satchel still bound shut with wax.

The Warriors may break the seals and read the dispatches, allowing them to look at the next three Dungeon cards and reorder them as they wish. Alternatively, they may keep the letters intact. If so, and they reach the next Settlement, each Warrior gains 1D6 x 25 gold from the grateful recipient. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A bundle of pitons, hammers and pry-bars lies neatly stacked beside a collapsed shaft.

The Warriors may keep the tools. The next time a Locked Door, Portcullis, Pit or Stone Block style event would hinder the party, the tools allow them to overcome it automatically with no delay. If unused by the end of the adventure, the tools may be sold for 75 gold. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Warp-lightning races through old brass conduits buried in the walls.

Randomly determine which Warrior is struck by the main discharge. He suffers 2D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. Then roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 the lightning arcs to the next nearest Warrior, inflicting 1D6 Wounds on him with no modifiers for Toughness or armour.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
Rune-carved stones set into the floor still show the measured craft of a vanished hold.

If the Warriors take time to read the waystones, they may look at the next three Dungeon cards and reorder them as they wish. A Dwarf automatically understands the markings. If there is no Dwarf in the party, any other Warrior must roll 1D6 and score 4, 5 or 6 to interpret the runes correctly. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A whisper not meant for mortal ears slips through the chamber and claws at the mind.

Each Warrior must roll 1D6 and add his Toughness, not including armour. If the total is 7 or more he resists the whisper and gains +1 to hit in the first round of the next combat against Undead. If the total is less than 7 he suffers 1 Wound, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour, but still learns enough to gain the bonus. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
An ivory warhorn hangs from a peg, polished by hands that must have died centuries ago.

The Warriors may sound the horn. If they do, no Unexpected Event is rolled in the next Power Phase, but the next Monsters they fight gain +1 Attack each in the first round as the blast echoes through the tunnels. If they leave the horn untouched, one Warrior may keep it and sell it for 100 gold at the next Settlement. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Bright green moss glows softly in a damp cleft in the stone, giving off a clean herbal scent.

Any Warrior may harvest and apply the moss. For each Warrior who does so, roll 1D6. On a score of 1 there is no effect. On a score of 2, 3, 4 or 5 he recovers 1D3 Wounds. On a score of 6 he recovers 1D6 Wounds and may also remove one temporary penalty affecting him. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
The ghost of a lean swordsman bows with exquisite courtesy and points his blade at the nearest Warrior.

One Warrior may accept the duel. Roll 1D6. On a score of 1, 2 or 3 he is outmatched and suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. On a score of 4, 5 or 6 he earns the spectre's respect and gains +2 to hit in the first round of the next combat. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
An ancient stone idol tilts forward as centuries of dust and rot finally give way.

Each Warrior on this board section must roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 he is struck by flying masonry and suffers 1D6 + 1 Wounds. Normal modifiers for Toughness apply, but armour offers no protection against the crushing weight of the stone.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
The flagstones split apart beneath a Warrior's boots, revealing a black drop below.

Roll 1D6 for each Warrior. The Warrior with the lowest score falls into a concealed pit and suffers 2D6 Wounds. He may escape in one turn if the party has a rope or access to Levitate. Otherwise he must spend his next turn climbing out and may do nothing else.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
Bloody prayers and ragged warnings have been scrawled across the wall by a penitent madman.

One Warrior may take the fanatic's lesson to heart and scourge himself for strength. He suffers 1D3 Wounds. If he does so, all Warriors gain +1 Attack in the first round of the next combat against Chaos or Undead. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A side chapel of cracked stone and dust-covered icons offers a rare sense of sanctuary.

If the Warriors spend one full turn in prayer or quiet rest, each Warrior recovers 1 Wound. In addition, one Warrior of the party's choice may recover a further 1D6 Wounds as he draws strength from the chantry. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Rusting cages line the walls and a handful of starved captives beg the Warriors for freedom.

Freeing the prisoners takes 1D6 turns. For each turn spent forcing locks and chains, there is an Unexpected Event in the Power Phase on a roll of 1 or 2 instead of only on a 1. If the Warriors survive the dungeon and escort the prisoners to the nearest Settlement, each Warrior gains 1D6 x 50 gold as reward. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A glaze of white creeps over the stones as unnatural winter grips the chamber.

Each Warrior must roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 he slips on the frozen floor and suffers 1D6 Wounds. On a score of 3 or 4 he keeps his feet but is slowed by the rime and is at -1 Move until the end of the next Warriors' Phase. On a score of 5 or 6 he avoids the trap.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A bubbling iron pot still hangs over a cold fire where Goblin shamans once brewed their vile concoctions.

One Warrior may drink the brew. Roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 he suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. On a score of 3 or 4 he recovers 1D6 Wounds. On a score of 5 or 6 he recovers 1D6 Wounds and gains +1 Attack in the next combat. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A gust rushes through the tunnels and a line of embers bursts into a wall of fire.
Each Warrior on this board section must roll 1D6. On a score of 1, 2 or 3 he suffers 1D6 + 1 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. On a score of 4, 5 or 6 he dives aside just in time.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
Transparent Dwarf craftsmen emerge from the walls, tapping at stone with tools that ring like distant bells.

If a Cave In or Portcullis currently blocks the Warriors' retreat, the ghostly masons clear it and then vanish. If there is no such obstacle in play, make a note that the next Cave In or Portcullis event drawn this adventure is ignored instead. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A long stone table is strewn with gnawed corpses, crude candles and silver goblets blackened by grime.

Any Warrior who searches the table must roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 he catches corpse-fever and is at -1 Move for the rest of the adventure. On a score of 3 or 4 he finds coins and rings worth 1D6 x 20 gold. On a score of 5 or 6 he finds healing herbs amongst the rot and recovers 1D6 Wounds. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A brass rune flares beneath the dust and the chamber suddenly feels like a furnace.

Roll 1D6 for each Warrior. The Warrior with the lowest score has triggered a concealed rune of Hashut. He suffers 2D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. In addition, the blast leaves him reeling and he is at -1 Attack until the end of the next Monsters' Phase.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A funerary urn lies broken, spilling perfumed yellow sand across the flagstones.

One Warrior may coat his weapon with the grave sand. The first successful hit he inflicts against an Undead model in the next combat causes +1 Damage. If no Undead are encountered before the end of the adventure, the sand may instead be sold in the next Settlement for 50 gold. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
The scrape of hidden gears is followed by the whistle of old Dwarf-forged blades.

Roll 1D6 for each Warrior. The Warrior with the lowest score is targeted by a battery of spring-loaded axes. He suffers 2D6 Wounds, with no modifier for armour except a shield or helmet. If more than one Warrior rolls the same lowest score, all of them are hit.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A tiny hiss in the darkness is the only warning before a poisoned dart strikes home.

Roll 1D6 for each Warrior. The Warrior with the lowest score is hit by a concealed Skaven dart and suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifier for armour. Roll another 1D6. On a score of 1, 2 or 3 the poison numbs his limbs and he is at -1 Move until the end of the next combat.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A blind hermit sits cross-legged beside a heap of bones, murmuring prophecies to no-one at all.

The seer offers the Warriors a single warning before fading into the shadows. The Warriors may look at the next two Dungeon cards and reorder them as they wish. In addition, during the next combat one Warrior may reroll a single missed attack. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Seven black candles burn with steady flames around a circle of old ash.

If there is a Wizard in the party, he may carry the candles' power with him and gains +1 on all casting rolls until the start of the next Power Phase. If there is no Wizard, one Warrior may instead keep the candles and sell them for 100 gold at the next Settlement. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Bloody handprints and snapped arrows show that a skilled hunter stalked some great beast through these halls.

The Warriors study the spoor and learn where to strike. One Warrior of the party's choice gains +2 to hit against the toughest Monster in the next combat. If that Warrior personally slays that model, he gains an extra 100 gold for the deed. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A ring of heavy old keys hangs from a peg hammered into a crack in the wall.

The Warriors may keep the keys. The next Locked Door, Portcullis or prison-style obstacle encountered this adventure is opened automatically. If no such obstacle appears before the end of the adventure, the keys may be sold for 75 gold in the next Settlement. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
The next doorway slams shut like the jaws of some enormous trap.

Roll 1D6 for each Warrior. The Warrior with the lowest score is caught by spiked iron shutters and suffers 2D6 Wounds, with no modifier for armour. He may do nothing until the start of the next Warriors' Phase as the others wrench the trap apart.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
An oathstone covered in soot and axe-nicks still bears the names of a lost Ironbreaker guard.

If the Warriors pause to touch the stone and swear to hold fast, the next Cave In or falling masonry effect encountered this adventure has no effect. In addition, during the next trap event each Warrior counts as having +1 Toughness against any Wounds suffered. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A small ivory bear hangs from a thong of blue leather near a heap of frozen bones.

One Warrior may take the charm. In the next combat, the first 2 Wounds he suffers are ignored as the spirit of Ursun hardens him against pain. Once used, the charm crumbles into frost. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Rows of hidden slits open in the wall with a dry metallic click.

Roll 1D6 for each Warrior. The Warrior with the lowest score is struck by a storm of knives and suffers 2D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for armour. If more than one Warrior rolls the same lowest score, each of them suffers the damage.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A black glass mirror framed in silver shows each Warrior a richer, stronger and more terrible version of himself.

Any Warrior may choose to gaze into the mirror. For each Warrior who does so, roll 1D6. On a score of 1 he suffers 1 Wound and loses 1D6 x 10 gold to the hungry glamour. On a score of 2, 3 or 4 nothing happens. On a score of 5 he may help reorder the next two Dungeon cards. On a score of 6 he gains +1 to hit in the next combat. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A barricade of shattered shields and splintered spear shafts marks where stout defenders met their end.

If the Warriors search the barricade respectfully, each Warrior gains 1D6 x 10 gold in loose coin. In addition, one Warrior of the party's choice takes a battered but serviceable shield boss and gains +1 Toughness against the first hit that wounds him in the next combat. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
An old man in a soot-black coat emerges from the dark, carrying a lamp that burns with a steady yellow flame.

The stranger silently guides the Warriors through a side passage before vanishing. The Warriors may look at the next two Dungeon cards and reorder them as they wish. In addition, if either card is an Event, they may discard it and draw the following Dungeon card instead. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A ragged visionary sways in the dark, muttering fragments of doom and victory in the same breath.

Roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 his ramblings waste precious time and there is an Unexpected Event in the next Power Phase on a roll of 1 or 2 instead of only on a 1. On a score of 3 or 4 his warning is useful and the Warriors may look at the next two Dungeon cards and reorder them. On a score of 5 or 6 he presses herbs and charms into the Warriors' hands, allowing one Warrior to recover 2D6 Wounds and granting a reroll on the next Monster Table result. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A lacquered merchant's coffer lies wedged behind a fallen beam, still locked tight.

One Warrior may try to open the box. Roll 1D6. On a score of 1 the box cracks loudly but holds nothing. On a score of 2, 3 or 4 it contains 2D6 x 25 gold. On a score of 5 or 6 it contains 3D6 x 25 gold and a fine cordial that restores 1D6 Wounds to one Warrior. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A dead sellsword still clutches a bloodstained map annotated with hurried curses and warnings.

The map shows enough of the dungeon to be useful before it falls apart in your hands. The Warriors may look at the next three Dungeon cards and reorder them as they wish. If one of those cards is an objective room, it must remain amongst the three cards but may be moved within them. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A slick patch of filth erupts into a mass of hungry black leeches.

Roll 1D6 for each Warrior. The Warrior with the lowest score is covered by the creatures and suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for armour. Until the end of the next Warriors' Phase he is at -1 Move as he tears them from his flesh. A healing potion removes this penalty immediately.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A stone relief shows Wolf Priests hurling back horrors from the gates of Middenheim.

The sight steels the Warriors' hearts. In the first round of the next combat, every Warrior gains +1 to hit. If that combat is against Chaos or Beastmen, each Warrior also gains +1 Attack in the first round. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Pale light gleams from a still pool that reflects a night sky far above, though no opening can be seen.

Each Warrior may drink from the well once. Roll 1D6 for each Warrior who does so. On a score of 1 he is chilled to the bone and suffers -1 Strength in the next combat. On a score of 2, 3, 4 or 5 he recovers 1D6 Wounds. On a score of 6 he recovers 2D6 Wounds. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A pale lantern burns with a graveyard light that no draft can extinguish.

One Warrior may carry the lantern until the end of the next combat. While he does so, Undead and Daemons may not pin any Warrior in the party. In addition, one Warrior of the party's choice immediately recovers 1D6 Wounds. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A painted jar sealed with resin sits untouched in a funerary alcove.

Breaking the seal reveals sacred oils from distant Nehekhara. One Warrior may anoint his wounds and recover 2D6 Wounds. In addition, the first successful hit he inflicts against Undead in the next combat causes +2 Damage. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A rotten rope snaps overhead and a spiked net crashes down from the roof.

Randomly determine which Warrior is caught in the net. He suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for armour, and is pinned. At the start of each Warriors' Phase he may cut himself free by sacrificing all his Attacks that turn, or an adjacent Warrior may free him by sacrificing 1 Attack.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A tattered black wolf banner leans against the wall, stubbornly refusing to rot away.

One Warrior may bear the standard into the next combat. While he does so, all Warriors gain +1 Attack in the first round of that combat. If the standard-bearer is reduced to 0 Wounds, the effect ends immediately. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
The remains of a long-dead pilgrim rest beneath a cloak covered in holy badges from across the Empire.

If the Warriors spend a turn laying the pilgrim to rest, each Warrior may take 1D6 x 10 gold in votive coins from the burial niche and no Unexpected Event is rolled in the next Power Phase. If the Warriors simply loot the body and move on, each Warrior gains the gold but there is an Unexpected Event on a roll of 1 or 2 in the next Power Phase instead. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A broken Skaven handcart lies on its side, packed with foul jars, mouldy wrappings and brass tubes.

The Warriors may search the cart. Roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2, a jar bursts and each Warrior on this board section suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. On a score of 3 or 4, the cart holds usable bandages and herbs, enough for two separate restorations of 1D6 Wounds each. On a score of 5 or 6, the Warriors find 2D6 x 25 gold and pungent repellents, so the next time Skaven are rolled on a Monster Table you may reroll once. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A rusted censer swings from a hidden chain and bursts open in a cloud of yellow gas.

Each Warrior must roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 he suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. On a score of 3 or 4 he suffers no damage but is weakened and is at -1 Strength until the end of the next combat. On a score of 5 or 6 he avoids the fumes.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A loose stone hides a cunningly made thief-box marked with the sign of Ranald.

One Warrior may try his luck with the cache. Roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 the compartment is trapped with a spring-latch and he loses 1D6 x 10 gold from his purse as the mechanism snaps shut. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 he uncovers 3D6 x 20 gold in stolen coin and a handful of marked tokens. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Thin wires whip out of the darkness and cinch tight around an ankle.

Roll 1D6 for each Warrior. The Warrior with the lowest score has stepped into a Skaven snare. He suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for armour, and may do nothing until the start of the next Warriors' Phase unless an adjacent Warrior spends 1 Attack cutting him free.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A sluggish river of black sewage blocks the way, carrying bones, broken shields and worse things besides.

The Warriors may cross carefully, which takes one extra turn, or they may rummage through the foul debris as they wade. If they search, one randomly determined Warrior finds 2D6 x 25 gold, but until the party is fully across the channel there is an Unexpected Event in each Power Phase on a roll of 1 or 2 instead of only on a 1. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A weathered rune-post stands in a recess where an underground stream once flowed.

The rune's old magic still holds a little power. No Unexpected Event is rolled in the next Power Phase. In addition, the next time a trap event is drawn, roll 1D6. On a score of 4, 5 or 6 that trap is ignored completely. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
An old crystal lens pivots in the ceiling and flashes with blinding light.

Each Warrior must roll 1D6. On a score of 1, 2 or 3 he is dazzled and suffers -1 to hit in the next combat. The Warrior with the lowest score also suffers 1D6 Wounds as the focused beam burns into his flesh, with no modifier for armour.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
Freshly painted warning runes in Khazalid point towards cracks and murder-holes in the walls.

The Warriors are forewarned and ready. The next time Monsters are revealed, they do not attack immediately, even if another rule would allow them to do so. In addition, each Warrior gains +1 Attack in the first round of that combat. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A ring of skulls set into the walls begins shrieking with the voices of the dead.

Each Warrior must roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 he suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. On a score of 3 or 4 he escapes physical harm but is shaken, suffering -1 to hit in the next combat. On a score of 5 or 6 he hardens his heart and ignores the terror.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A mortared stone door marked with old funerary symbols has somehow remained untouched.

The Warriors may break the seal and investigate. Roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2, choking dust pours out and each Warrior on this board section suffers 1 Wound, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. On a score of 3 or 4, the ossuary contains funerary masks and rings worth 2D6 x 20 gold. On a score of 5 or 6, the Warriors recover blessed salts and gain +1 to hit in the first round of the next combat against Undead. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A polished black stone the size of a fist rests in a cradle of old bronze.

The stone offers a brief glimpse ahead. Before the next Dungeon card is drawn, the Warriors may inspect it. After seeing it, they may place it back on top of the deck or move it to second place. In addition, one Warrior may reroll a single missed attack in the next combat. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A white marble basin still holds clear water untouched by the dungeon's filth.

If the Warriors spend one full turn tending their hurts at the font, each Warrior recovers 1D3 Wounds. In addition, one Warrior of the party's choice may remove a temporary penalty to Move, Strength or to hit. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A black altar bearing the raven sigil of Morr stands untouched in the gloom.

If the Warriors spend one full turn in silence before the shrine, the god of the dead grants them uneasy peace. No Unexpected Event is rolled in the next Power Phase, and one Warrior of the party's choice recovers 2D6 Wounds. If the Warriors disturb the shrine instead, nothing happens save the sour smell of old incense. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A cracked reliquary of beaten silver bears the twin-tailed comet beneath a crust of old wax.

If the Warriors kneel and recite a prayer to Sigmar, one Warrior of the party's choice recovers 2D6 Wounds. In addition, in the first round of the next combat against Chaos or Undead, every Warrior gains +1 Attack. If the Warriors break open the reliquary for valuables, it contains nothing but dust. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A cracked monastery bell hangs over the chamber, yet when it moves no sound comes forth.

Touching the bell stills the dungeon for a while. No Unexpected Event is rolled in the next Power Phase. In addition, until the end of the next Warriors' Phase the Warriors may move normally and are not subject to pinning by wandering creatures or swarms. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Filthy claw-marks and greasy symbols cover the walls where ratmen once argued over stolen routes.

The Warriors may use the markings to avoid danger. If they do, discard the next Dungeon card drawn and replace it with the one after it. However, each Warrior must also lose 1D6 x 10 gold as unseen Skaven fingers pluck coins and trinkets from their packs while they study the wall. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A low cairn of orange-crested helms and rune-marked stones stands at the crossing of two tunnels.

One Warrior may swear a Slayer's oath before the cairn. In the next combat he gains +1 Strength and +1 Attack, but if he is able to move he must always move towards the nearest Monster. If he survives the combat, the party finds 150 gold in old offerings wedged between the stones. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A loose block reveals a dry niche crammed with contraband, lamp oil and travel gear.

The Warriors may choose one of the following benefits: either recover 1 Wound each as they share the provisions, or take goods worth 2D6 x 25 gold to divide amongst the party, or keep the rope and tools found here so that the next Pit-style trap encountered this adventure has no effect. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A mocking voice repeats the Warriors' own words back at them until the sound turns murderous.

Each Warrior must roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 he suffers 1 Wound, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. If there is a Wizard in the party, the sorcerous resonance also inflicts -1 on all his casting rolls until the start of the next Power Phase.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
Fat cave mushrooms grow in a churned patch of earth littered with gnawed bones and squig droppings.

Any Warrior may eat one of the mushrooms. For each Warrior who does so, roll 1D6. On a score of 1 he suffers 1D6 Wounds. On a score of 2, 3 or 4 there is no effect. On a score of 5 he recovers 1D6 Wounds. On a score of 6 he recovers 1D6 Wounds and gains +1 Attack in the next combat. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A small golden idol sits on a pile of mouldering sacks where thieves clearly meant to return for it.

The Warriors may return the idol to the nearby niche or steal it. If they return it, no Unexpected Event is rolled in the next Power Phase and one Warrior recovers 2D6 Wounds. If they keep it, the party gains 150 gold but there is an Unexpected Event in the next Power Phase on a roll of 1 or 2 instead of only on a 1. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A treacherous stair gives way beneath a Warrior's weight with a crunch of broken stone.

Roll 1D6 for each Warrior. The Warrior with the lowest score traps his leg in a collapsing step and suffers 1D6 Wounds. Until the end of the next Warriors' Phase he may not move unless an adjacent Warrior spends all his Attacks helping him free.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A cool woodland breeze stirs the dust, carrying for a moment the scent of leaf and rain.

The blessing of some distant wood settles briefly upon the Warriors. In the next combat each Warrior gains +1 Move and +1 Attack in the first round. In addition, the next pit or collapse effect encountered this adventure has no effect on the party. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Antlers and weathered oak leaves have been arranged into a shrine to the god of wild places.

If the Warriors leave the shrine undisturbed and spend one turn listening to the dark, they gain a hunter's calm. Until the end of the next combat each Warrior is at +1 Move, and the next Monsters revealed do not attack immediately. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Soft feet patter just beyond the lantern light and a small shadow darts between broken pillars.

Each Warrior must roll 1D6. The Warrior with the lowest score loses 1D6 x 20 gold to an unseen thief. If another Warrior chooses to give up his next turn chasing the culprit, the stolen gold is recovered and the thief drops a healing draught that restores 1D6 Wounds to one Warrior. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A dead mercenary captain still grips a sealed contract and a purse heavy with advance pay.

The Warriors may take up the contract. If they do, nominate one Warrior as paymaster. If the paymaster survives the adventure, each Warrior gains 1D6 x 50 gold in the next Settlement when the contract is honoured. If the paymaster is reduced to 0 Wounds at any point, the contract is lost. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A crude booth of planks and bones blocks the way, daubed with Goblin glyphs demanding payment.

Each Warrior may pay 50 gold to pass quietly. If every Warrior pays, no Unexpected Event is rolled in the next Power Phase. If the party refuses, roll 1D6. On a score of 1, 2 or 3 hidden bomb charges explode and each Warrior suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. On a score of 4, 5 or 6 the booth collapses to reveal a cache worth 2D6 x 25 gold. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A dead brazier suddenly stirs with blue flames as cold as winter in Middenheim.

One Warrior may thrust his hands into the embers and take Ulric's ferocity into himself. In the next combat he gains +1 Strength and is immune to Fear. If there are no enemies before the adventure ends, the blessing is simply lost. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
An ornate coffin of black wood stands in an alcove draped with moth-eaten velvet.

The Warriors may pry it open. Roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2, a swarm of bats bursts out and each Warrior on this board section suffers 1 Wound, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. On a score of 3 or 4, the coffin holds rings and brooches worth 2D6 x 25 gold. On a score of 5 or 6, it contains a vial of dark elixir that restores 2D6 Wounds to one Warrior, but he is at -1 Toughness in the next combat. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A domed chamber of polished stone is covered from floor to roof with names and sworn grudges.

If every Warrior spends one full turn in silence speaking his name and purpose, the ancient hall answers with a low rumble. In the first round of the next combat, all Warriors gain +1 Attack and are not subject to pinning. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Silver nails and scraps of scripture have been hammered into a low archway ahead.

The first Warrior to pass beneath the arch gains its protection. The next spell-like effect or trap effect that would inflict Wounds on him this adventure is ignored completely. In addition, the next Monsters revealed do not attack immediately. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
Blue-white light crawls across the walls as a cracked wardstone discharges its last strength.

Each Warrior must roll 1D6. On a score of 1 or 2 he suffers 1 Wound, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour. If there is a Wizard in the party, the magical backlash also causes him to suffer -1 on all casting rolls until the start of the next Power Phase.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
Carved faces stare from every wall, their gem eyes turned towards the lantern flame.

If the Warriors extinguish all light and wait in darkness for one full turn, a hidden panel opens. The party may inspect the next Dungeon card before drawing it, and each Warrior recovers 1 Wound as cool air flows from the secret passage. If the Warriors do not wait, the chamber remains silent and closed. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A tiny carved leaf marks a crack in the wall where only Elven eyes would normally look.

The sign reveals a safer route. The Warriors may look at the next two Dungeon cards. If either card would reveal Monsters, they may discard that card and draw the following Dungeon card instead. No treasure is gained for completing this event.
A faded hex-mark on the threshold flares green as the Warriors pass over it.

Randomly determine which Warrior draws the brunt of the curse. He suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or armour, and is at -1 to hit in the next combat as the sigil burns in his sight.
Roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 draw another Event card immediately.
A heavy wolf-headed helm rests on a broken pedestal, its eye-slits still bright with cold purpose.

One Warrior may don the helm for the next combat. In the first round he gains +1 Toughness and +1 Attack. If he is able to move, he must always move towards the nearest Monster while wearing it. No treasure is gained for completing this event.

Your Warrior's wild behaviour attracts the attention of the Settlement authorities and there are thrown outoftown.They must wait outside the Settlement gates for the other Warriors. In addition,roll1D6.On a score of1 your Warrior is fleeced by the gate guards asthey are thrown outand have alltheir gold stolen!

In one of the wharf side bars, your Warrior meets a young entrepreneur with great ideas for establishing a trading empire tocover the Known World.Hetalksyour Warrior into investing 1D6x5o gold per Battle-level, giving them a certificate of purchase. From now on,atthe end of the Dungeon,wheneveryour Warrior reaches civilisation,they may visit the local Merchant's Guild andshow them theirdeedofpartnership(thistakesone day). Consulting their records,they can tellyour Warrior how the companyisdoing.Roll1D6 on the following table: 1 Thecompanyhasgonebust,andyourWarrior's investment is worthnothing.In addition,they mustpay the merchants1D6x50goldperBattle-levelin outstanding debts. 2-5 Your Warrior's investment is performing fairly well and the merchant forward them 1D6x10goldperBattle- level as their share of the profits. Businessisbooming!Afterconsultingtheledger,the 6 merchant confirms that he can forward your Warrior 1D6x50 goldperBattle-level.

Your Warriorisadoptedbyasmalldog.Itfollowsthem around everywhere,skulkingin theshadows while the fighting goes on and then emerging after the adventure is completed to shower its new master with adoration.Rollonthe Pet Dog Event Table (page 24) to see exactly which type of canine has attached itself to you. Your Warrior can killthe dogifyou want,butitwouldbe a cruel and heartless thing to doandit will cost them 1,ooo gold topayforadecentburialforthehound! Ifyour Warrior already hasapetdog,ignore this event and roll again on the City Events table.Ifnot, name it and noteit downon your Warrior's Adventure Record Sheet. 18 llfind a whole array ofthings to do! There willbe more more chances of something great happening butalso more a in ment and more people to meet.A crow dedcity willalso t m ore...
As your Warrior rushes through the busy streets, their money pouch is stolen. YourWarriorloses1D6x50gold.

Your Warrior spends the rest of their day in the heady atm o sphere of a steam bath,sweating off the aftereffects of last night's sojourn at the tavern. They emerge feeling far healthierthantheyhaveforagoodwhile,and theymayadd+1 Wound to their Starting Wounds scorepermanently.Inaddition, they are +1Initiativeforthe next Adventure.
See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

Your Warrior encounters a nervous looking merchant who is in a bitofa bind and asksforhelp.Someofhis workers have failed toshow up for work and he has a lot of crates that need to be transported to the warehouse district. Roll 1D6 on the table below: 1 The crates contain stolen goods and the Watch has had themerchantundersuspicionforsometime.Theentire group, including your Warrior, is arrested. Your Warrior mustspendthenext2 days in jail during which they are cleared of any wrong doing.Unfortunately,they fail to getpaid. 2-3 Your Warrior does not realise that the crates are filled with illegal substances and is surprised when the Watch arrives to arrest the merchant.Obviously,your Warrior receives no pay. 4-5 Your Warrior works diligently andis paid 50 goldfor a hard day's work. 6 Your Warrior's efforts are exemplary and the task earns them 1oo gold.
Taken with the joys of hot food and a comfortable bed after so long outin the wild,your Warrior overspends on such luxuries by 50 gold.

Your Warrior is invited by a group of locals to goon a nocturnal hunt. Meeting them just before sunset,they are informed that tonight's prey is the great Qu arg a beast of fearsome temper andfouldisposition.Your Warrior's task in the group is to take thehuntingtools-anet,asmallpolewithabellonitandabag full of garlic and wait in the middle of the woods while the rest of the hunters spread out into the forest and drivethe Quarg towardsthem.Asthesunrisesthenextmorning,and theowls return to their nests, there is stillno sign of the Quarg,your Warrior begins to wonder if someone is being made afoolof...

Your Warrior is captivated by a particular brilliant team of street artists, who are performing their own rendition of a popular and contemporary play. The play your Warrior is watching iscalled (rollonce on each of thetablesbelow): 1D6 Result 1 Death of... The Lost Love of... 2 A Return To... 3 4 No Solace For... Cursed Be... 5 6 Drunken As... 1D6 Result 1 ...theEmperor. ...aLove Struck Wastrel! 2 3 ...Solitude. ...the Creeping Dead. 4 5 ...the Sylvan ian Counts. ...the Ever hungry Halfling! 6 Rollanother1D6toseehow good the actors were: 1 Appalling! Your Warrior has never seen such rubbish in their life and pelts the unfortunate actors with rotten vegetables! 2-5 Entertaining! Your Warrior gives the actors 10 gold x the total of the numbers rolled on the tables above. 6 Splendid! Your Warrior is captivated and showers the actors with well-earnedpraiseandalotofgoldtoo! Your Warrior gives the actors5o gold xthe numbers rolled on the tables above.

As your Warrior attempts to buy someequipment,the trader points out their gold is counterfeit and refuses to take it. Looking through their stash, your Warrior discovers that 1D6x10 of their gold is indeed worthless. Discard it and crossit off your Adventure Record Sheet. Ifyour Warrior does nothavethatmuchgoldleft,andhas already spent some in theSettlement,they must leave forthe nextDungeonimmediatelybeforeanyonecatcheson.

Studying the weapons on display in the local smithy, your Warrior feels a steel gauntlet ed hand upon their shoulder.Itis the Marshal of the Watch and your Warrior is accused ofacrime they did not com mit! Your Warrior is arrested and thrown uponthe mercy of the courts. Your Warrior must roll once on each table below to see what crime they are accused ofand what their sentence is. 1D6 Crime 1 Trouble making 2 Robbery 3 Forgery 4 Arson Treachery 5 6 Murder Sentence 1D6 1 Innocent A stern warning! 2 3 1000 gold fine Lose a limb* 4 10 years in jail** 5 Deathby(rollontheExecution MethodTable) 6 1D6 Execution Method 1 Burning 2 Beheading 3 Impalement Drowning 4 5 Torture 6 Firing Squad *Lose a Limb The authorities decide to chop offone ofyour Warrior's limbs as punishment. You may choose which.If theylose aleg,your Warrior's Movement is halved and their Escape from Pinning roll is always a 6+.Ifthey everlose another leg they maynot move atall. If they lose a hand,thentheyare-2 onallTo Hit rolls and they may neveruse missile weaponsorcastspells.Iftheyever lose another hand they may not fight at all. **10YearsinJail This effectively means your Warrior is out of the game (as indeed does ' sentenced to death by...'.However,atthe start of every Adventure from now on,roll1D6.Onascoreof5or6 your Warrior escapes,together with all their gear, and may rejoin their fellow Warriors. Buying Your Freedom Ifthe sentence is a 4,5 or 6,the arrested Warrior(and other Warriors) can bribe the guards and the court officials in orderto avoidharm.Thecostofthis will be found by adding together all of the dice rolls from the Crime/Sentence/Execution Method tablesandmultiplyingtheresultby1oo.Thatishow muchgold it will cost for your Warrior to w alkfree,unharmed. 19

"The Slammer" has come to town and he is taking on all challengers in a 'friendly' wrestling match! Boasting a perfect record,theNorseman wagers5oogoldpiecestoanyonein the crowd who defeats him in unarmed combat. At the urgingof the crowd your Warrior accepts the challenge,roll2D6 and apply their Alehouse modifier (see your Warrior's Rulebook). Look uptheresultonthetablebelow: 2-3 In the first few seconds,TheSlammerpicksyour Warrior up and promptly smashes them to the ground. The sickening thud ofyour Warrior hitting the ground sendsashudderthroughthecrowd.Momentslater your Warrioriscartedoffonawoodenplank.Notonly isyour Warrior5o0gold pieceslighter,butThe Slam mer helps himself to oneitem of Treasure (determined atrandom)your Warrior was carrying. 4-5 Aftera nifty maneuver,The Slam mergrabs your Warrior in a bear hug that feels like they're being squeezed by...well,abear. With eyes bulging your Warrior gives up just prior to slipping into unconsciousness. With terribly bruised ribs and an even more bruised ego yourWarriorforfeits5o0gold. 6-8 After a grueling 15 minutes of dirt eating exchanges between The Slam mer and your Warrior,the matchis calleda draw.Your Warrior gains nothing butthe knowledge that they lasted longer than most and has lost nothing morethan afew teeth... +6 With all the focused fury of a hungry Half ling in apie shop your Warrior meets The Slam merheadon.After several minutes of intense combat your Warrior takes the measure of his foe and slams the Norseman to the ground!Standingtriumphantamidstacheeringcrowd, your Warrior dusts off their clothing before marching off with 5o0 moregold pieces in their pocket.

On any other score,the vial actually does contain a magical holy water, purified and blessed by a priest.If drunk, the imbibe r is protected by a magical aura which lasts for one Turn and all attacks against the drinker made by Undead Monsters do half the normalDamage(rounded down).If thrown atan Undead Monster rollon your Ballistic Skill,each successful hit causing1D6 Wounds with no deductions for anything.

Slumped againstthe trunk of an old tree sits the sad remains of aonceproudwarriorwith acrumplednoteclutched inhis cold, dead hand. Trudgingthroughthewilderness, oneofthe Warriors (determine randomly)inadvertentlysteps intoa foot noose. In theblink of an eye, the foot noose hauls the Warrior high up into a tree. The Warriorsattempttocrossintoanewareabutarestopped bythelocalmilitiawhoinsistthateach Warriorpay 5 goldfor eachitemof Equipmentand Treasurethattheyarecarrying. The Warriormust sell Equipment and Treasure if necessary. If they cannotpay, the guards take as much as theyhavebefore letting thempass.

Onthesideoftheroad isanelaboratetombstonewith one of the Warrior's nameengraveduponit! Each Warriormustroll 1 D 6. The name on the tombstone is thatof the Warriorwith the lowestscore. " AWarriormaythrow 1 D 6 x 25 goldintothewellonetime, whereuponitmysteriouslyvanishesandthe Warriormaymake rollonthefollowingtable: The Warriorgainsnoknowledge. 1-2 E The Warriorreceivesinsightintoaconceptofefficient movement, gaining+1 Initiativeforthenext Adventure. 4 The Warriorunderstandstherelationshipbetweena Warrior and theirweapon. The Warriorsstrayoff themainroadandhappenuponan abandoned stonecottage. Theinterioris quaint andfurnished withchairs andevenbeds. The Warriors are soelated tofind a comfortableplacetorestthattheyfind it hardtoleave the place. The Warriorsdecidetorelaxandenjoythecomfortofthe cottage, addingoneweektotheirjourney. Theyarewell-rested, however, andeachofthemgain+1 willpowerand+Initiative untiltheendofthenext Adventure.

Trekkingacrossashallowstream the Warriorssee anancient stonearchwaycoveredinmossandvines. As theypass throughthestructurethe Warriorspromptlydisappear, rematerialisingnearastreamthatthey'veneverseenbefore. Afterchecking theirmapwith thesurroundinglandmarks, they determinethattheyaresomehowclosertotheirdestination thantheywerebefore. Thearchwayportalthattheycame through is nowhere tobe seenbuttheirjourneyisreducedby oneweek. Each Warriormustroll 1 D 6 andif thescoreisa‘1'itmeans thatoneofthe Warrior'sitemsof Equipmentor Treasure (determinedrandomly)didnotmake itthroughwiththem. Discard itimmediately. . EVENT
These dirty but well-fashioned weapons were scavenged from who knows where. Some have crude runes or sigils cut into them, and they all look lethal.

Roll 1D6 to see what has been found: 1 A halberd (BE) 2 A warhammer (BDE) 3 An axe (BD) 4 A flail (BD) 5 A mace (BD 6 A sword (BDEW) Which... Croll 1D6): 1 Causes +1 Wound per Battle-level. 2 Ignores 1D6 points of Armour. 3 Gives the wielder +1 To Hit. 4 Gives the wielder +1 Attack. 5 Gives the wielder the Parry 6+ ability. 6 Causes +1D6 Wounds on a To Hit roll of 6. Now roll again. If the roll is higher than the first score to see what the weapon does, the weapon has the second ability as well and so on. Each ability adds 200 gold to the weapon's value. ROLEPLAYING GAME ONLY PERMANENT
This scruffy-looking backpack is of the kind often used by adventurers.

In the backpack there are 1D3 items of Dungeon Room Treasure. The Warrior who gets this card can Keep all 1D3 items for themselves, and they only count as one item for the purposes of divvying out Treasure. USE THEN DISCARD
Anurandel was a Wood Elf Sorceress, renowned for her peaceful demeanour, sunny disposition and hatred of battle. She fashioned many items to aid her followers should they be assailed by dark forces.

When a Warrior invokes the power of this amulet, Monsters only hit the wearer on a natural To Hit roll of 6. After the first successful hit, the amulet's power wanes and Monsters will hit as normal. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Charadris was a Priest of Sigmar who abhorred magic in all its forms, believing that his faith in Sigmar was the greatest power of all.
Any Undead Monsters with the Regenerate ability adjacent to the Warrior wearing this amulet may only regenerate half the number of Wounds they normally would (rounded down). PERMANENT

At the heart of this crystal amulet a magical fire rages. This amulet allows a Warrior who is not a Wizard to cast a spell. Take one of the Wizard's Starting Spell Cards at random. The Warrior may try to cast the spell once per Turn, rolling equal to or over its Casting Number on 1D6 to do so. After successful use, the amulet disintegrates. DISCARD AFTER SUCCESSFUL USE
This ornate amulet throbs dully, and a thick, black liquid appears to flow within its core.

At the start of each Warriors' Phase the bearer may roll 1D6. If the score is a 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6, they get +2 Strength for the rest of the Turn. If the score is a 1, the amulet explodes causing 1D6 Wounds to the bearer, with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. LASTS UNTIL EXPLODES
This antiquated porcelain amulet is covered in a layer of cracked and peeling black leather.

This amulet allows the Warrior to counter hostile magic. Each time a spell is cast against the bearer of the amulet, roll 1D6. On a score 6 the spell has no effect on them (RPG: A Warrior wearing this amulet gains the Magic Resistance 6+ ability). PERMANENT
These ancient documents are faded and somewhat fragile.

A Warrior who reads these documents must roll 1D6 to see what they reveal: 1 A recipe for Halfling pie. 2 A very old letter from a noble, worth 10 gold. 3 A musical masterpiece from a famous bard, worth 25 gold. 4 Battleplans from ages past, worth 50 gold. 5 A religious text, worth 100 gold. 6 A Treasure Map that leads to 500 gold! (RPG: The Warrior must add two extra weeks of travel to a Settlement after which they uncover the gold.) DISCARD AFTER SALE OR USE
Lustria is a land of the unknown, the undiscovered continent, a realm of Giants and Daemons and powers beyond all telling.
This vase is of strange design, unsuitable for use by Humans, Elves or Dwarfs. Its purpose can only be guessed at, as can its age, but its beauty and value is beyond question. DISCARD AFTER SALE
This silver dust swirls in its casket as if shifted by an unseen finger.

A Warrior who consumes this powder has their movement increased. They may immediately move another 3D6 squares, and automatically Escape from Pinning. Arkal's Powder may be consumed at any time. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This armour gleams in a rainbow of never-ending array of carnage and war.

This armour can absorb Wounds inflicted on the Warrior. Once donned, the armour cannot be removed. It can absorb a total of 30 Wounds, then explodes, taking the wearer to zero Wounds (and death if no healing is possible). LASTS UNTIL EXPLODES
A thick black liquid drips ominously from the end of this arrow. The magical venom on this arrow can fell even the toughest monster.
If this arrow hits a Monster, roll 1D6. On a score of 1, 2 or 3 the arrow causes normal Damage. On a score of 4, 5 or 6 the Monster is immediately slain. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The shafts of these jet-black arrows are encrusted with golden runes and their tips glow with a pale green light.
These enchanted arrows are magically sharpened and cause +3 Wounds when they hit. There are 1D6 Arrows of Death. Each arrow can only be used once. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
These magically sharpened white arrows are able to pierce the toughest armour at a thousand paces.

A Warrior using these arrows must roll 1D6 after each successful hit. On a score of 5 or 6 (6 if the Armour is magical) the arrow ignores the target's Armour when determining Damage. There are enough arrows to last one Adventure. LASTS FOR ONE ADVENTURE
These arrows were skillfully crafted by the Wood Elves of Athel

Loren. Arrows of Sure Flight may only be used to their full effect by an Elf. When used by any other Warrior treat them as normal arrows. An Elf using these arrows gets +1 To Hit. There are 2D6 arrows. Each arrow can only be used once. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
This axe retains a drop of blood from every foe the wielder has defeated.
A Warrior wielding this axe gets +1 Attack. PERMANENT
This axe of Dwarf origin was created to punish foes for crimes they have committed.
A Warrior using this axe gets +1 To Hit. PERMANENT
This axe was forged by the Dwarf Runesmith Garvan
Surehammer, who honed its blade to an impossible sharpness. This axe causes +1D6 Wounds. PERMANENT
This rugged backpack is of the kind favoured by those who venture into dangerous places.

The Backpack contains 1D6 sets of Provisions. Each set of Provisions restores 2 Wounds. (RPG: At the end of each Adventure, any remaining provisions in the Backpack are lost. However, at the start of the next Adventure, the Backpack will have magically replenished itself, and will contain 1D6 Provisions again!) 1D6 PROVISIONS PER ADVENTURE
This decaying bag is tied with a leather thong. Inside of it is a number of gold pieces.
The bag contains 1D6x25 gold. RECORD THEN DISCARD
This slender gold band glitters with a clear light.

When a Warrior finds this ring, randomly select two Attack Spells (RPG: randomly select two Attack spells with Casting Numbers that are equivalent to the Warrior's Battle-level). These two spells are stored in the magic band. Each Spell may be cast once, automatically, whenever the wearer wishes. When both spells have been cast, the magic power of the band has been used up and it crumbles to dust. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
Simple battlefield bandages used by dungeon adventurers.
Each bandage restores 3 Wounds. Use once then discard.
This tattered old scroll bears the seal of a renowned Bretonnian
Lord. Reading aloud the words on this scroll will kill any single Undead creature worth up to 400 gold on a dice roll of 4, 5 or 6. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Anurandel was a Wood Elf Sorceress of Loren, renowned for her soft voice, peaceful demeanour and hatred of battle.
This innocuous looking bauble is in fact an item of great power. This bauble flashes with energy and its bearer may add +1 to all dice rolls to Escape from Pinning. PERMANENT
Smoke incessantly curls from the hollows of this helm.

The Beasthelm protects the wearer from only the most powerful blows. Any Damage Dice that scores a natural 6 against the wearer of the helm causes only half the Damage G.e. 3 Wounds instead of 6). PERMANENT
The head of a vicious wolf glows on the front of this belt.

Each time a group of Monsters who all attack a single Warrior (RPG: Monsters who have the Gang Up ability) appear on the same Board Section as the wearer of this belt, roll 1D6. If the score is a 3, 4 or 5, the beasts attack the Warriors using the rule of One-On-One instead. If the score is a 6, the beasts attack each other in the first round of Combat instead, after which they attack the Warriors using the Rule of One-On-One. PERMANENT
This belt is adorned with the ferocious face of a lion, flanked by glowing runes of power.

A Warrior who dons this belt must roll 1D6 at the beginning of each Turn to see what the belt confers: 1 -1 Strength and -1 Toughness. 2-3 +1 Strength 45 +1 Toughness 6 +1 Strength and +1 Toughness The belt cannot be removed for the rest of the Turn. PERMANENT
This brutal weapon appears to be crudely fashioned from a dull iron-like metal, and has sinister-looking runes engraved upon its hilt.
When used by the Barbarian, this sword adds +1 to the dice roll to see if he becomes Berserk. When used by other Warriors, this sword has no magical effect. PERMANENT
The liquid in this bottle burns the Warrior's throat, and they experience an uncontrollable desire to kill their enemies.

When drunk, this potion lasts for an entire Combat. Each Turn, before they fight, the Warrior must roll 1D6, adding +1 to the score for every slain Monster on their Adventure Record Sheet. If the total score is 6 or more, the Warrior goes Berserk until the Combat is over. While Berserk, they get +1 Attack. If they roll a 1, however, the Warrior is so wild that all they do this Turn is slash about, causing 1 Wound to every adjacent Warrior. PERMANENT
This sword has been magically sharpened to a razor-fine edge and is capable of biting through steel as if it were paper.
This sword ignores all non-magical Armour (and up to 2 points of Magical Armour) when rolling for Damage. PERMANENT

Any bolt placed into this crossbows turns freezing cold. This crossbow is Strength 4 and any quarrel fired from it is considered magical. When using this crossbow a Warrior gets +1 To Hit and ignores up to 2 points of non-magical Armour. PERMANENT
This artefact was used by an Arabyan Knight in the 1000 year long 'War of Death'. The power of the amulet can be invoked against one Monster each Turn.

That Monster suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. The power of the amulet will regenerate after one use when there are no Monsters on the Board. If used twice without having regenerated, however, the amulet disintegrates after the second use and must be discarded. LASTS UNTIL DISINTEGRATES
This is the blade that the master swordsmiths of Bretonnia forged to defeat an undead Liche-king many centuries past.

Each Undead Monster in a square adjacent to a Warrior wielding this sword automatically suffers 1 Wound at the end of each Turn, after any Regeneration, with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. PERMANENT
This sword seems to have a life of its own, striking in a blurred arc of slicing metal.
A Warrior wielding this sword gets +1 Attack. PERMANENT
Forged from gold taken from the depths of the sea when the world was young, this blade is covered in runes so ancient and worn that they can no longer be read.
This sword ignores 1 point Armour when determining Damage. PERMANENT
This sword moans softly as it is drawn from its scabbard, in eager anticipation of the blood it is about to spill.
These unusual boots have stiletto blades concealed in the toes.
While a Warrior is wearing these boots, they gain a special Kick Attack which suffers -1 To Hit but is resolved at +1 Strength. PERMANENT
This huge ruby has been empowered with warding spells engraved into it by the wizards of the Empire.
Once per Turn, the Blessed Gem can be used to dispel a Necromantic spell. When the spell is cast, roll 1D6. On a roll of 4, 5 or 6 the spell has no effect. PERMANENT
Cooled in the water of the Temple of Ulric, this sword is a powerful weapon of purity.
While a Warrior is using this sword, they only miss their opponent if their To Hit roll is a 1 or 2. PERMANENT
The Blessed Water can be used to heal, exorcise evil spirits and banish Undead.

A Warrior who drinks some Blessed Water has 1D6 Wounds restored. If the result is a 1, however, it means that the Blessed Water has run out; discard it. The water can also be thrown as a missile weapon. Any Daemonic or Undead creatures hit suffer 2D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. If a Warrior throws the Blessed Water, it must be discarded. DISCARD ON A ROLL OF 1
The Warrior's sword glows as a thick red liquid coagulates on the blade.
Roll 1D6 for each Monster adjacent to the Warrior bearing the scroll. On a score of 5 or 6 that Monster suffers 1 Wound with no modifiers for anything. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This dark blade glows with an internal pulse.

The sword drinks the blood of its victims, causing +1D6 Wounds every time it hits. However, if the wielder rolls a 1 To Hit, the blade takes the energy it needs from the Warrior instead causing 1D3 Wounds with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. PERMANENT
A dull throbbing fills the room as it becomes suffused with a red glow.
The Warrior bearing the scroll immediately has 1D6 Wounds restored. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Warrior's outline shimmers and becomes an indistinct, hazy blur that is continually changing.
Pick any Warrior on the Board. All Attacks made against the chosen Warrior suffer -1 To Hit for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Faded runes are inscribed upon the pitted surface of this grubby looking circlet of rusted iron.
A Warrior wearing this crown gets +1 Toughness. Bograt's Krown can be worn at the same time as a helmet. PERMANENT
This magical blade is made from a bleached white substance that looks like bone, but is as sharp as steel.

A Warrior may use this sword once per Adventure. If their Attack hits, the sword causes + 1D6 Wounds. (RPG: For one Turn per Adventure, the Warrior may trade in all their normal Attacks and use this sword to make a single Attack that causes normal Damage + (1D6 per Battle-level) extra Wounds.) USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE

Turgit was as seemingly unstoppable Chaos Lord finally defeated by a group of Empire Battle Wizards who simultaneously cast their spells on him. The result was so powerful that the magic became fused into his bones.

A Warrior may use this bone by rolling 1D6. If the score is a 6, every Chaos creature adjacent to the Warrior suffers 1 Wound (RPG: per Battle-level) with no modifiers for anything. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This book contains powerful incantations that manipulate the

Winds of Magic. The Wizard may use this book just after the Power Phase has been rolled in an attempt to change the amount of Power that is available to him this Turn. Roll 1D6. The result is now the amount of Power that he has for the Turn. Note that this does not change the Power Phase. If the Power Phase roll was a 1, an Uneventful Event still occurs (RPG: and the Wizard may not add his Battle-level to the result). USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The pages of this book are initially blinding to the eyes. Eventually the light dims and the book can be read.
A Warrior who reads this book may add +1 to their Weapon Skill Characteristic permanently. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Anurandel was a Wood Elf Sorceress, renowned for her peaceful demeanour, sunny disposition and hatred of battle.

She fashioned many items to aid her followers should they be assailed by dark forces. These boots give the wearer the ability to dodge missile fire. Whenever a Warrior wearing these boots is attacked with a missile weapon, roll 1D6. On a score of 6 the attack misses. (RPG: The boots give the Warrior the Dodge 6+ ability against missile fire.) PERMANENT
The steel toes of these leather boots are emblazoned with a Magic rune in the shape of a key.

A Warrior wearing these boots may attempt to kick open any closed door by rolling 1D6. If the result is a 5 or 6 66 if the door is magically locked), the door opens. Only one attempt may be made per door and any traps on the door are set off as normal. PERMANENT
These sturdy iron-shod boots are magically made to be an exact fit for whoever wears them.
A Warrior wearing these boots gets +1 Weapon Skill and +1 Ballistic Skill. PERMANENT
These boots are fashioned from the tough leather of a Wyvern's wings.

These boots allow the Warrior to leap 1 square in any direction as part of their Movement, landing in the square beyond. Any obstacle in the square being leaped is ignored, though it still counts as 1 square of Movement. USE ONCE PER TURN
These boots are made of a strange, soft leather.

One per Adventure the Warrior wearing these boots gets +2 Movement for one Turn. (RPG: If the Warrior tries to sell these boots, no self-respecting trader will take them seriously and as such they cannot be sold.) USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
These dusty boots are made of a strange material. When worn, the world seems to blur and slow down.
While wearing the Boots of Swiftness a Warrior gets +1 Movement. PERMANENT
This long dead sorcerer Baalga the All-Seeing created these boots to enable his long suffering henchman Tzchak to walk long distances carrying immensely heavy loads.
These boots allow the wearer to ignore any Armour-based Movement penalties. PERMANENT
This mighty bow was crafted by the Elves of Tiranoc from a single Starwood branch.

When the land of Tiranoc was engulfed by the sea, the bow was carried to the Old World, a fabled treasure of a fallen kingdom. This bow is Strength 2 and causes +3 Wounds. It may be fired twice per Turn. PERMANENT
This white bow was captured by the Dwarfs at the fall of the

Elf city of Tor Alessi. Its accuracy is beyond compare, and it was considered a prize worthy of the bloodshed and slaughter that won it. This bow is Strength 4 and, rather than having to move then attack as usual, the wielder may fire it before, during or after their Movement instead. PERMANENT
This slim golden band fits comfortably around the wearer's forearm, even on top of armour.
A Warrior wearing this bracelet gets +3 Starting Wounds. PERMANENT
This plain bronze bracelet has a single rune embossed on its surface.

A Warrior who dons this bracelet may take on the appearance (and only the appearance) of any creature. While transformed, the Warrior will not be attacked and may automatically Escape from Pinning as long as they do not initiate combat. As soon as the Warrior attacks a target in any way the bracelet's effects wear off. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The runes on this blade shimmer in the light. It is exceptionally light and well-balanced in the hand.
A Warrior wielding this sword gets +2 Initiative. PERMANENT
This sturdy wooden barrel bears the mark of the great Dwarf

Master Brewer Josef Bugman. This is a barrel of the fabled Bugman's XXXXXX beer! There is enough beer for four drinks. If a Warrior takes a drink of the beer, they have 1D6 Wounds restored, but they suffer - 3 To Hit for the next 1D3 Turns. Every drink still left in the barrel is worth 50 gold. DISCARD WHEN ALL DRUNK
This horn was removed from a ferocious Minotaur and then fashioned into an instrument. Imbued with a strange magic, the horn seems to invigorate those who hear it.
When this horn is blown, each Warrior on the Board gets +1D3 Strength. The effect lasts until a 1 or 2 is rolled in the Power Phase. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Smoke rises from the bottom of this candle like spirits searching for something they cannot seem to find.

Once lit, this candle will illuminate the Board Section the bearer is on, just like the leader with the Lantern. It is not bright enough to be used to Explore through a Doorway. If the bearer of the candle flees the Dungeon, they do not need to roll on the Escaping the Dungeon Table. LASTS FOR ONE ADVENTURE
The point of this curved blade gleams with a viciousness that only a dirty, rotten sea scoundrel could appreciate.

A cutlass is more effective when used with finesse rather than brute strength. A Warrior wielding this sword adds their Weapon Skill to the Damage rather than their Strength. In addition, this magical cutlass causes +1D6 Wounds. PERMANENT
Overcome by an unreasoning madness, the Monsters leap towards each other in a berserk killing frenzy, completely ignoring the Warriors.

Roll 1D6 for every Monster on the same Board Section as the bearer of the scroll. On a score of 5 or 6 that Monster moves towards the nearest Monster and attacks it this Turn instead of the Warriors. If there is any confusion about which Monsters are attacked, the bearer of the scroll decides. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This golden casket emits a soft hum which continuously raises and lowers in pitch.

If they do nothing else this Turn, the Warrior bearing this casket may attempt to use it to capture a single spell of their choice from an adjacent enemy spellcaster. Roll 1D6. If the score is a 4, 5 or 6, the casket absorbs the spell and prevents the spellcaster from casting it again. (RPG: If the destroyed spell is rolled on a Monster's Spell Table when determining which spell it casts, roll again.) The Warrior may automatically cast the captured spell once during Combat. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Moaning in anguish, the Warrior spreads their arms as an area of blackness takes shape around their foes.
Pick any 2 square by 2 square area on the same Board Section as the bearer of the scroll. Every Monster in the chosen area suffers 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for anything. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Chalice of Fate has the power to warp time, to change fate, and to alter reality.
A Warrior may use the Chalice of Fate to re-roll the Power Phase roll. They may only re-roll once, and must accept the result of the second roll even if the score is worse. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This chalice is one of the legendary artefacts created by the

Priest King Alcadizzar. Much sought after by Witch Hunters, the chalice is filled with a golden liquid, which never dims or dries up. During Combat the Warriors may inhale the pungent vapours that rise from the bubbling golden liquid. For the duration of the Combat, on a roll of 6 To Hit, the Warriors will cause +1D3 Wounds. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Inside a small pouch is a modest amount of chalk, the kind that adventurers take into maze-like dungeons.

This chalk allows a Warrior to mark an escape route out of the Dungeon. The Warrior does not need to roll on the Escaping the Dungeon Table should they wish to leave the Dungeon. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This innocuous gourd is filled with a foul-smelling paste.

A weapon that has been smeared with this paste causes +1 Wound (RPG: +1 Wound per Battle-level) against Chaos creatures it hits. There is enough of the bane to coat one weapon for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Donning this charm brings everything into sudden clarity.

This beautiful charm is worth 100 gold. (RPG: This charm allows a Warrior to assimilate the knowledge required for training very quickly indeed. It costs them 300 less gold (per Battle-level) than usual to train to the next Battle- level.) USE ONCE THEN DISCARD

The bearer of this charm becomes very lucky indeed! Anytime the bearer of this charm finds gold whilst adventuring in the Dungeon, they discover an extra 1D6x10 gold hidden in the area. (RPG: In addition, whenever the bearer of this charm participates in gambling or other games of chance, they may add +1 to their score.) PERMANENT
A chest filled with sparkling gold coins is a welcome sight to any
Warrior. The chest contains 1D6x100 gold. RECORD THEN DISCARD
Repanse de Lyonesse was a Bretonnian Paladin famous for her assault on an invading Chaos army.
A Warrior wearing this crown gets +1 Strength The Circlet of Repanse cannot be worn with a helmet. PERMANENT
