This band of silvered, luminescent metal, worn around the forehead, glistens and gleams with an unnatural light. This circlet allows the wearer to enter a destructive blood rage.

Upon entering this state the Warrior doubles their regular Attack Characteristic for one Turn and must roll 1D6. On a score of 1 the enraged Warrior must move towards the nearest Warrior and attack them instead. While in this state the Warrior may automatically Escape from Pinning. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This cloak is made from a fabric that seems impossible to tear.

A Warrior who wears this cloak gets +1 Toughness. However, if any Damage Dice from an opponent scores a natural 6, the cloak ceases to provide any more benefit (RPG: until the beginning of the next Adventure). PERMANENT
This cloak swirls around the Warrior as they move, deceiving the eye as to their precise location, and causing their opponents to become ensnared in its all enveloping folds.
All Attacks against the Warrior wearing this cloak suffer -1 To Hit. If an attacker scores a 6 To Hit, however, the power of the cloak is exhausted (RPG: until the next Adventure). PERMANENT
This cloak catches the light in a very strange manner, becoming transparent in places as if it were full of holes.

While wearing this cloak the Warrior becomes invisible and therefore cannot be attacked. They may fight as normal. The cloak contains enough power to keep the Warrior invisible for one Turn per Adventure. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This cloak is made of a fine, silk-like material that, although extremely light, is very, very strong.

Every time a Warrior is hit while wearing this cloak, they can ignore the first Wound caused by the blow. However, each time the cloak absorbs Damage like this, roll 1D6. On a score of 1 the cloak rips and is useless. PERMANENT
This cloak is made of an odd material that is slippery to the touch.
While a Warrior is wearing this cloak they may automatically Escape from Pinning once per Adventure. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This magical cloak is woven with leaves, animal hide and bird feathers.

A Warrior who wears the cloak to jump up into the air for one Turn before slowly settling back to the ground. While in the air, the Warrior may not engage in hand-to-hand Combat but any missile Attacks they make get +1 To hit, while any missile attacks against them suffer -1 To Hit. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The scroll bears the words for the Confuse spell. The arcane words create confusing images around the Monster's head, and it stumbles around befuddled.
Pick any Monster on the same Board Section as the Warrior bearing the scroll. That Monster suffers -1 Attack this Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This jewel contains the power of the Coughing spell. When used, the Warrior's opponent suddenly bursts out in a hacking cough of such violence that they look as if they may drop their weapon.

Pick any Monster on the same Board Section as the Warrior with the jewel and roll 1D6. On a score of 6 the target succumbs to a coughing spasm and suffers -1 To Hit for the rest of the Turn. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Solid rock leaps from the Warrior's outstretched fingertips, building a bridge across the room.

When cast, this spell allows the bearer to create a magical bridge 1 square wide across any pit, hole or chasm. The bridge remains in place until a 1 is rolled in the Power Phase, at which point it suddenly disappears. Any models on the bridge at that time plummet into whatever the bridge was crossing. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This crown pitches the wearer into a world of insane lunacy.

This valuable gold crown is worth 250 gold. (RPG: Cackling and giggling to themselves at their own macabre jokes, the Warrior wearing the crown finds the death and carnage in the Dungeon most amusing. They now get +1 to all psychology based tests such as Fear and Terror.) PERMANENT
This jet black crown is fashioned from bands of iron and engraved with powerful runes that crackle and spit with an incandescent blue light.

Each time a spell is cast against the Warrior wearing this crown, roll 1D6. On a score of 6 the spell has no effect on them (RPG: the crown gives the wearer the Magic Resistance 6+ ability.) PERMANENT
This crown was worn by Rhag Hellroar who became a great

Norse Chieftain. The wearer of this enchanted crown gets +1 Attack and +1 To Hit. If any of their To Hit rolls score a natural 1, however, the wearer may not make any offensive actions during the next Turn, and gets +2 Toughness instead. PERMANENT
This magical crown was created long ago by an Ice Witch of Kislev and is said to contain some of her magical power.
When the wearer invokes the power of the crown, for one Turn all Attacks made by the wearer cause +3 Wounds and the weapon they are using is treated as magical. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This scroll bears the words for the Cure Light Wounds spell.
When the Warrior uses the scroll, pick any Warrior on the Board and roll 1D6. On a score of 6 the spell restores 1 of their Wounds. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The life-like features on this small figurine are _ quite disconcerting.

A Warrior may toss this magical figurine into the air during Combat, where it will grow in size and hover above the Monsters. In the next Monster's Phase every Monster on the Board will attack the figurine instead of the Warriors. The figure can absorb a total of 20 Wounds before it is destroyed and the Monsters' attention turns back to the Warriors. If it survives the Combat, it may be used, but note how many Wounds it has left to absorb. LASTS UNTIL DESTROYED
This dagger imbeds itself in the target, refusing to be removed.

A Warrior may attempt to plunge this dagger into any adjacent Monster at any time by rolling 1D6: 1 The Warrior accidentally stabs an adjacent Warrior (determined at random) who suffers 2D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Armour. 2-5 The Monster suffers 2D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Armour. 6 The Monster suffers 2D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Armour. At the start of each Turn thereafter the Monster suffers 1D3 Wounds with no modifiers for Armour. The dagger may be retrieved at the end of the Combat. USE ONCE PER COMBAT

Tucked within a velvet sheath, this small dart is more than it seems... In addition to any other attacks they may have, a Warrior may throw this dart as a missile weapon with a Range of 6 squares. The Warrior suffers -1 To Hit if the target is 4 squares away, -2 if the target is 5 squares away, and -3 If the target is 6 squares away.

If it hits, the dart causes 1D6 Damage with no modifiers for anything. The dart painfully embeds itself into the Monster and the creature suffers -1 To Hit for the rest of the Combat. The dart may be retrieved at the end of the Combat. USE ONCE PER COMBAT
These two daggers of purest steel were forged in the time of the High Elves. Ancient sigils bind powerful spells of destruction to their blades.

While using the Darting Steel Daggers a Warrior gets +1 Attack and each Attack made with the daggers automatically hits. However, each hit will only cause 1D6 Wounds, with no modifiers for Strength. PERMANENT
A blurring screen of energy shoots towards the target, engulfing it in a maze of glittering colours.
When used, the bearer of the scroll may pick any Monster on the board. For this Turn, all hand-to-hand Attacks made against the Monster only miss on a natural To Hit roll of 1. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Deathstone is a small black crystal that seems to soak up the light from the lantern, converting it into raw power.

The Deathstone acts as a source of Power for the Wizard. When casting a spell, he may use the Deathstone to increase his chance of success. Roll 1D6. On a 3, 4, 5 or 6, the spell may be cast at a cost of zero Power instead of its normal cost. On a 1 or 2, the spell fails and all the Wizard's Power for the Turn is lost. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This sword's power is such that it cannot really be controlled and would soon overwhelm whoever carried it.
A Warrior using this sword may increase their Strength to 10 for one Turn. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This piece of crisp white parchment rustles and crackles, silver lettering shifting across the page.

This scroll allows the bearer to automatically dispel any one spell cast against them or their companions. That spell has no effect. (RPG: The spell is also destroyed, preventing the caster from ever casting it again. If a destroyed spell is rolled on a Monster's Spell Table when determining which spell it casts, roll again.) USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Warrior bearing the scroll raises their hand and shouts the words of warding, diffusing their opponent's magic.
The Dispel Magic scroll may be used to attempt to dispel another spell that has just been cast. Roll 1D6. If the score is 4, 5 or 6 that spell has no effect. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
White power streams from the eyes of the Warrior bearing the scroll, forming a protective dome around the Warriors.

This scroll allows the bearer to protect up to 4 squares of the Board with an impenetrable magic shield for one Turn. The squares must be linked together, but the dome can be of any shape. Models inside the dome may not move, take offensive action or cast any other magic, but are immune to all forms of attack. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
A heavy stone, carved in the shape of a ferocious beast, rests at the end of this flail.

While a Warrior is using this flail, they get +2 Strength. If the wielder's To Hit roll is a natural 6, the flail causes Double Damage. If the wielder has a Weapon Skill less than 5 (including items, special abilities, etc.) and rolls a 1 To Hit, they hit themselves causing normal Damage. PERMANENT

These durable iron spikes are the kind used to wedge doors shut. A spike keeps a door closed for one Turn. To use a spike, a Warrior must be adjacent to a door. Once spiked, a door cannot be opened unless it is broken down. For someone to break down a door, they must be adjacent to it.
Roll 1D6 and add their Strength. If the total is 9 or more, the door is broken down. There are 1D6 spikes. Each spike can be used once. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
This shield is fashioned from the deep-red, sulphurous scales of a Great Fire Dragon.
A Warrior bearing this shield gets +1 Toughness. Once per Adventure they may use the shield to ignore all of the effects from any one attack, whatever its origin. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE

A fire suddenly erupts from the end of this rod, scorching all who come in contact with it. The rod may be used at any time against one Monster adjacent to the bearer. It causes 2D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Armour. (RPG: If used against a Mummy, the creature is also set alight.

AT the start of each Monsters' Phase thereafter the Mummy suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. If the roll is a 1 when determining Damage, the fire goes out and the Mummy suffers no more fire Damage. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This ancient Elven helm is inscribed with a glowing rune of fire.
While a Warrior is wearing this helmet they get +1 Toughness (RPG: or +2 Toughness against any fire-based attacks). PERMANENT
The Warrior mutters the words on the scroll and gestures towards the ground, causing the target's grip on its weapon to fail.

Pick any Monster on the same Board Section as the bearer of the scroll. The target drops any one object they are holding. The Monster may do nothing for one Turn as they retrieve it. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD

This tightly sealed wooden tube protects a valuable parchment. The tube contains an ancient map of a Dungeon. From looking at the map, the Warriors can see that there is a secret door leading out of one of the rooms.

On the next new Board Section that the Warriors enter, place an extra Doorway on one of the free walls. Through the Doorway is a single Dungeon Room. When the Warriors enter the room, take 2 Event Cards. Once the Events are completed, the Warriors gain two items of Dungeon Room Treasure in addition to any they gain for completing the Eents. USE THEN DISCARD
This old but serviceable pick looks like the ones used by Dwarf miners.

A Warrior may use the pick to dig their way through a cave-in. It takes 1D6 Turns to clear a way through the rubble. After each successful use, roll 1D6. On a roll of 1 the pick breaks and must be discarded. LASTS UNTIL DESTROYED
This Dwarf warhammer glows with a pale blue light.
This magical Warhammer causes +1 Wound. PERMANENT
This nutritious, delicious, cake-like food is what Elves take with them on long journeys. There are 1D6 pieces of Elf Waybread. Each cake restores 1 Wound to the Warrior who eats it.
At the end of each Adventure, roll 1D6 for each piece of Waybread. If the score is a 1, that piece has spoiled and must be discarded. DISCARD WHEN ALL EATEN
This bow is enchanted with Elf magic that makes it much more accurate.
This bow is Strength 4. While a Warrior is using this bow, they get +1 To Hit. PERMANENT
This enchanted rope was commissioned by a long-dead Emperor from far-off Nippon to assassinate his foes. It was also the instrument of his death...

The bearer may direct this rope to strangle any Monster on the Board. Roll 1D6. If the score is 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6, the Monster suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. If the score is a 1, the rope attacks the Warrior instead! At the beginning of each Turn thereafter, roll 1D6. If the total is equal to or greater than the victim's Strength, they suffer another 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. If the total is less than the target's Strength, they manage to free themselves. USE ONCE PER COMBAT
The Fay Enchantress of Bretonnia placed a spell upon this bulb of garlic to help a Bretonnian Knight in his battle against a monstrous Vampire.

Any Vampire attacking a Warrior bearing this garlic suffers -1 To Hit. (RPG: At the end of each Adventure, roll 1D6. If the score is a 1, then the enchantment has worn off and the garlic must be discarded.) PERMANENT
This magic shield expands and swells whenever the bearer is being assaulted.

A Warrior bearing this shield gets +1 Toughness. Each time the bearer is hit they may use it to try and absorb the blow. Roll 1D6. On a5 or 6, the shield absorbs all the Damage. On a roll of 1, the shield explodes and the Warrior takes 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. LASTS UNTIL EXPLODES
This glowing jewel, set in a silver clasp, stores powerful magic.

This jewel contains 6 points of Power that the Wizard can use in the same way as his Power Tokens. Place a D6 on top of this card to show the jewel's current Power. Once all the points are used up it is useless and worthless. It cannot be recharged. The jewel is worth 50 ogld per unused point of Power. DISCARD WHEN DRAINED
This blade of pure iron was tempered to the sound of arcane incantations in ages past. This weapon's magic guides the wielder's hand, making it easier to strike foes.
While using the Ensorcelled Blade, a Warrior gets +1 To Hit. PERMANENT
These exquisite sweetmeats from Tilea are preserved in tightly sealed stone containers.
These foodstuffs are a rare delicacy, and are worth 50 gold. DISCARD AFTER SALE
This shoulder pad once belonged to the late, great Blood Bowl
Star Player Bozark 'The Boz' Bile-Belly. This piece of Blood Bowl memorabilia is worth 300 gold. If worn by an Ex-Blood Bowl Player, it causes +2 Wounds. PERMANENT
This helm is fashioned into the likeness of the gaping maw of a ravenous beast.

A Warrior wearing this helm gets +1 Toughness. In addition, they may nominate one of their Attacks each Turn. If that Attack hits, then the Warrior gains an extra bite attack from the helm which hits the same target automatically, doing 1D6 Damage with no modifiers for anything. PERMANENT USE ONCE PER TURN
In the Kingdoms of Ind, strange land of fabulous temples, dwell some of the most exotic beasts in the world.
These are the feathers of mystical birds from Ind, the Land of a Thousand Gods. There are 1D3 sets of feathers and each set is worth 1D6x10 gold. DISCARD AFTER SALE
This drawstring bag is filled with colourful treats.

Festival Treats can be eaten at any time but they are very hard and durable so each time a Warrior (except a Dwarf) tries to eat one, they must roll 1D6. If the score is 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6, the Warrior has 1 Wound restored. If the score is a 1, the Warrior painfully breaks a tooth on the treats and suffers 1 Wound instead. There are 2D6 treats and each is worth 10 gold. DISCARD WHEN ALL EATEN
These well preserved clothes would not look out of place in the wardrobe of a royal household.
Having been carefully stored, these fine clothes are made of silk and inlaid with gold and jewels. There are 1D6 items, and each is worth 1D6x25 gold. DISCARD AFTER SALE
Connoisseurs throughout the Old World speak with reverence of the exquisite wines made by Elves. There are 1D6 bottles of wine, and each is worth 100 gold.
If a Warrior drinks a bottle of wine, they have 1D3 Wounds restored but suffer -1 To Hit for the next 1D3 Turns. DISCARD AFTER USE
This dusty scroll contains magical sigils that are traced in a spidery scrawl. It is the Finger of Doom spell - ancient and powerful magic.
While he has this scroll, the Wizard may take the Finger of Doom Spell Card and use it just like any of his other spells. PERMANENT
The Warrior points at one of their companions and a thin stream of blue energy pours from their fingertips, revitalising them.
Pick any Warrior on the Board and roll 1D6. On a score of 4, 5 or 6 the chosen Warrior has that many Wounds restored. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
These regular arrows have been doused in oil and can be easily lit before firing.

If these arrows hit, roll 1D6. If the score is a 4, 5 or 6, the target is set alight and suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. The Damage continues each Turn until the Damage roll comes up a natural 1 or 2 and the fire goes out, causing no more Wounds in subsequent Turns. There are 1D6+3 Fire Arrows. Each arrow can only be used once. DISCARD AFTER ALL USED

This vial contains a pale red liquid that sparkles as it catches the light. It is the harsh and fiery liquid used by Orcs to heal their wounds. This is supposed to be a healing potion and holds enough liquid for four doses. Each unused dose is worth 100 gold. Whenever a Warrior takes a drink of the potion roll 1D6: 1 The potion tastes awful, and does no good at all other than induce a fit of vomiting!
2-4 The potion tastes pretty bad, searing the gullet, but quickly restores 1D6 Wounds. 5-6 The burning brew restores 1D6+1 Wounds (RPG: +1 Wound per Battle-level). DISCARD WHEN ALL DRUNK
From the rune inscribed on it, the fuse jutting out of the top, and its pungent smell, this large, spiked ball can only be a firebomb.
The Firebomb may be thrown at any time and affects an area 2 squares by 2 squares. Any model in the area suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Prising the skull-marked lid off a wooden box, the Warriors uncover a stash of firebombs!

A Firebomb may be thrown at any time, and affects an area 2 squares by 2 squares. Any model in the area suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. There are 1D6 Firebombs and each Firebomb can only be used once. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
The Warrior conjures up a huge iron-mailed fist and sends it hurtling towards their opponent.

Pick any Monster up to 6 squares away from the bearer of the scroll and roll 1D6. If the score is equal to or greater than the range of the target, it suffers 1D6+1 (RPG: 1D6+Battle-level Wounds. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD

At the end of each chain on this flail is a gnashing, biting, chattering skull! A Warrior using this flail gets +1 To Hit. Once per Adventure one of the Warrior's Attacks causes Triple Damage. PERMANENT USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This flail was used by a Bretonnian Knight in his battle against an army of Chaos.

A Warrior using this Flail gets +1 Strength. In addition, the Warrior may nominate one of their regular Attacks each Turn. If that Attack hits with a natural 6, treat it as 1D3 separate hits. Roll the Damage for each hit separately. PERMANENT USE ONCE PER TURN
This small, sturdy bag contains some notoriously volatile flash powder.
The Flash Powder may be set off at any time to confuse the Warriors' enemies. After it has exploded, any Warriors who have not yet fought this Turn get +1 Attack. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD

A volatile, magical fire rages inside of this thick flask. This flask may be thrown into any square, where it explodes into a ball of fire. Roll 1D3. The score indicates how many D6 Damage are inflicted on the model in the square, with no modifiers for Armour. The score is also the number of squares in every direction that the ball of fire expands to. The fire dissipates outward, with each square away from the centre taking a D6 less Damage than the previous, with no modifiers for Armour.

See diagrams below: Aad IP 186/100) D205 208 108 Sieur Deas.. mle oe ae 'Pe! pa aegis 2D6 36 ees xs fe (D6, 1D6\<)) BS. 2D6 2D6/1DG} a pete "a wes a pet 3 1D6 Damage 2D6 Damage 3D6 Damage 1 square 2 squares 3 squares USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The label on this red-tinted bottle is unreadable and the liquid within swirls mysteriously.

Any arrows or bladed weapons coated in this poison cause +2 Wounds on the next successful hit. There is enough poison for 1D6 applications, each of which last 1 Turn. Each unused application is worth 50 gold. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
This scroll bears the words for the Fleet of Foot spell. When cast, the bearer of this scroll feels light and swift
Upon using this scroll the Warrior gets +1 Movement for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The scroll bears the words for a Flesh Worm spell. When cast, a small glowing worm appears, hurls itself at a Monster and burrows into its flesh.

Pick a Monster on the same Board Section as the Warrior with the scroll. The Monster is attacked by a Flesh Worm and suffers 1 Wound with no modifiers for anything. At the start of every Turn thereafter, roll 1D6. On a score of 1, 2, 3 or 4 the Flesh Worm dies. On a score of 5 or 6 it continues to feed and the victim suffers another 1 Wound. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This enchanted white flute was carved from a branch of the rare white oaks of Loren.
When blown, the flute allows all of the Warriors on the same Board Section to re-roll one of their dice rolls this Turn. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
These yellow-stripped trousers are decorated with magical glowing runes.

A Warrior who wears these trousers can fly for one Turn. While airborne, they can fly over all obstacles and automatically Escape from Pinning. Anyone who attacks them suffer -1 To Hit. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
A freezing wind swirls through the room, chilling the Monsters to the bone yet leaving the Warriors unharmed.

Roll 1D6. The number shows two things: 1 The number of Monsters who may be affected by this spell. 2 The amount of Wounds each Monster affected suffers, with no modifiers for anything. The targets of this spell must be on the same Board Section as the bearer of the scroll. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This jewel is carved of ice that never melts and is freezing to the touch.

When its power is invoked, it releases a lethal blast of icy shards at the Warrior's foes. This jewel may be used to cast the Freezing Death spell. The bearer makes an attack roll as if using a missile weapon. On a successful hit roll 1D6 to work out the jewel's Damage. If the score is a 1, the jewel causes 1D6 Wounds. If the score is a 2, the jewel causes 2D6 Wounds, on so on. USE ONCE PER TURN
The slimy green potion in this flask turns Night Goblins into gibbering Fanatics.

There is enough liquid in the flask for four doses. A Warrior can take a drink at any time during Combat. Roll 1D6 to see what happens: 1-2 The brew makes the Warrior hallucinate! They immediately make an attack against every model adjacent to them. 3-4 The brew restores 1D6 Wounds. 5-6 The brew restores 1D6 Wounds and the Warrior may ignore the Damage from the next blow they take. The brew is worth 200 gold if none of it has been drunk, or 100 gold if some of it (but not all of it!) has been consumed. DISCARD WHEN ALL DRUNK
Rolled up and placed over one arm, this thick fur cloak offers some protection in place of shield.

If a Warrior uses this cloak instead of a Shield, they get +1 Toughness. Each time the Warrior wearing the cloak is hit, roll 1D6. If the roll is a 4, 5 or 6 it remains effective and can be used again. If the roll is a 1, 2 or 3 it is ruined and must be discarded. LASTS UNTIL DESTROYED
Carried long ago by the Witch Hunter Hernando Hellman, this magic item was used to subdue evil-doers so that they could be brought back to stand trial for their crimes.

This gavel may be thrown as a missile weapon but the Warrior throwing suffers -1 To Hit. Anything struck by the gavel is rendered unconscious for the next 1D3 Turns. If the To Hit roll is a natural 1, however, the gavel mysteriously vanishes (discard it). Further attacks against an unconscious target only miss ona To Hit roll of 1. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE

The Gem of Passage was wrought many centuries ago by the Dwarfs. It aided them in building their underground empire and can be used to control the very rocks that make up the ceiling and floor of the dungeon.

When a Warrior invokes its power, the Gem of Passage opens up a hole in one of the walls. Place another Doorway on the Board Section the Warrior occupies. Beyond this Doorway will be 1D6+3 more Dungeon Cards which the Warriors can explore as normal. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This gem was crafted by a clever Halfling who always seemed to avoid being selected for perilous errands outside the Moot.

If the rules require a Warrior to be randomly determined for any reason, the bearer of this gem may decide which Warrior is selected instead. The first time the gem is used, turn this card upside-down; when it Is used a second time, flip this card over. USE TWICE PER ADVENTURE
Every component in this powder was blessed by a priest of Sigmar and then enchanted by a wizard.

A Warrior may use this powder to coat one of their weapons. In the first Turn when it is applied, the weapon causes +2D6 Wounds against any Undead. During the second Turn, it causes +1D6 Wounds. In the third Turn it causes +1D3 Wounds, after which the effects wear off. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This massive sword may only be wielded with both hands and even then it takes a mighty Warrior to use it effectively.
While a Warrior is using this two-handed sword they get +3 Strength. PERMANENT
The scroll bears the words for the Glittering Robe spell. The

Warrior bearing the scroll creates a robe of magical energy that shimmers and glitters about them as they move. This spell creates a cloak of magical energy around the bearer, which absorbs the next 1D6 Wounds they would suffer, after which it fades away. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Globe of Detection allows the Warrior's vision to pierce the solid rock of the dungeon walls.

A Warrior with this globe can easily find hidden passages. When the Warrior rolls on the Hidden Passages Table, they mad add +1 to their roll. However, if the Warrior rolls a 1, then that result stands and the Warrior will suffer the 'Collapse!' result. USE ONCE PER TURN GLORY! SCROLL (DEW) The Warrior bearing the scroll sends out tendrils of force that pierce the hearts of the Warriors, filling them with great valour and courage beyond measure. All of the Warriors are suddenly enthused with great bravery and each gets +1 Attack this Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Warrior bearing the scroll sends out tendrils of force that pierce the hearts of the Warriors, filling them with great valour and courage beyond measure.
All of the Warriors are suddenly enthused with great bravery and each gets +1 Attack this Turn. Use once then discard.
This small crystal orb holds the light of the dawn, giving off the same light as the Lantern.

Once activated, the orb glows brightly and the bearer can explore in the same manner as the leader with the Lantern. How long the Glowstone will last is unknown, however, and if the Power Phase roll is a 1, roll a further 1D6. On a score of 1, 2 or 3 the glow dies and the Glowstone is useless and worthless. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This goblet is suffused in a brilliant golden aura which expands to fill the area, flooding it with bright light.
When the power of this cup is used, each Warrior on the Board Section has 3 Wounds restored. << USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This dull grey armour is made of a flaky, stone-like substance and is extremely resilient.

Whenever a Warrior wearing this armour is hit by an Attack, roll 1D6. On a score of 1 the armour shatters and is useless and worthless. On a 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6 the armour absorbs that many Wounds. LASTS UNTIL DESTROYED
This mace seeks the death of those around it.

While a Warrior is using this mace they get +1 Strength. Once per Adventure, if a Warrior forgoes all of the regular Attacks this Turn, every adjacent model (friend or foe) suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. PERMANENT USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The age-pitted surface of this ancient stone hides the power to drain the life energy of others and transfer it to its wearer.

Upon invoking the Grimstone choose any other Warrior in the party. The bearer now has the same number of Wounds as the Warrior, while they now have the number of Wounds the bearer had. In effect, their Wounds are swapped. The Grimstone may never be used to take a Warrior above their Starting Wounds, which remain as they were. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This magical hammer was constructed to help settle grievous offences against Dwarfs.
While a Warrior is using this weapon, they may re-roll the first Attack that misses each Turn. PERMANENT
This black-bladed axe exudes a poisonous, corrosive slime. It is rumoured to be the axe stolen from Chaos Warlord Grulmak Deathmonger by the - notorious' Hobgoblin Scragface Throatslasher.

This axe ignores 3 points of Armour, and spatters acid everywhere. At the end of the Warriors' Phase, every model adjacent to the wielder suffers 1 Wound with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. However, every time the wielder rolls a 1 To Hit, they are struck by the acid themselves and suffer 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. PERMANENT
The Warrior bearing the scroll casts a glowing halo around themselves.

This spell surrounds the Warrior with a protective shield. At the start of each Turn roll 1D6. The result is the number of Attacks that can be absorbed by the halo this Turn. Ignore all of the Damage from those attacks. The halo remains until the bearer moves or a 1 is rolled on the dice to see how many Attacks are absorbed. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This magical hammer was forged by a devoted follower of Morr, the God of the Dead.

A Warrior using this hammer causes Double Damage if their To Hit roll is a natural 6. In addition, both the wielder, and any Monster hit by the hammer (even if it causes no Damage), can only restore half the number of Wounds from any healing that they receive this Turn. (RPG: nor can they use the Regenerate ability if they have it this Turn.) PERMANENT
This sturdy Dwarf warhammer has an ornate icon carved on its head. Any Dwarf in the party recognises this as the lost Hammer of Hargon.

When using this hammer a Warrior may roll as many dice for Damage as they like. Add up the total, then add the Warrior's Strength to the final score. However, if any of the dice come up with a 1 or 2, the hammer causes no Damage at all. PERMANENT
The face of this hammer is so hot, that it heats up any surface it touches. This hammer causes +1D6 Wounds.
If the target is wearing non-magical Armour, however, the hammer causes +2D6 Wounds instead. PERMANENT
This staff calls out warnings during combat, alerting the wielder to incoming attacks.
A Warrior wielding this staff gets +3 Toughness. (RPG: The staff is cumbersome in combat, however, and the Warrior suffers -1 Attack to a minimum of 1. PERMANENT
This jewel contains the power for the Healing Hands spell. When used, the Warriors feel a soothing calmness descend upon them.
When the power of this jewel is used, every Warrior on the Board has 1 Wound restored. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Inside this tightly sealed glass jar is a small quantity of a liquid that can only be a healing potion.
A Warrior who drinks this potion has 1D6 Wounds restored. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Although ominous in appearance, the thick, yellow paste contained within this black bottle is a salve of healing.
The healing salve can restore 2D6 Wounds to any single Warrior, or 1D6 Wounds to each of 2 different Warriors. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
As the Warrior utters the words on this scroll, power surges through the target's body and their heart beats with renewed Vigour.
Pick any Warrior on the Board. That Warrior has 2D6 Wounds restored. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This sword has the ability to warp time, guiding its own destiny to the benefit of whoever wields it in battle.
Once per Turn, while a Warrior is using this sword, they may re- roll any one of their Attacks that misses. PERMANENT
Sparks fly from the iron-shod head of this mace as it is wielded, bathing the area in light.
While using the Hell Mace a Warrior gets +2 Attacks. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The Warrior assumes the aspect of a daemonic creature.

When used, the Warrior bearing this scroll gets 1 Attack, +1 To Hit, causes +2 Wounds and cannot use a weapon. (APG: In addition, they are immune to all psychological effects such as Fear or Terror, for one Turn). USE ONCE THEN DSICARD
This helm protects the wearer against the arcane arts.

A Warrior who wears this helm gets +1 Toughness. In addition, they may attempt to absorb any spell cast on them by rolling 1D6. If the score is a 2+, the helm absorbs the spell and the Warrior suffers no effects. If successful, the next time the Warrior uses the helm to absorb a spell they need a 3+ and so on. This continues up to 6+, after which the cycle starts all over again. If the score is ever a 1, the helm explodes and the Warrior suffers 2D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE

After a thorough search, one of the Warriors finds a secret panel which opens, revealing a hidden alcove. To see what the alcove contains, the Warrior may roll as many dice as they like. If any score a 1 they have found nothing, and managed to make a lot of noise - take an Event card immediately. If any come up with a 2, 3, 4, or 5, the Warrior gains gold equal to the total of those dice multiplied by 10. For each dice that scores a 6, they receive an item of Dungeon Room Treasure.
This ornate artefact obviously belongs to one of the temples of the Empire.

At the end of the Adventure roll 1D6 to see whether you find a temple. On a score of 4, 5 or 6 you find a temple and sell the artefact for 1D6x100 gold. (RPG: You may find a temple in any Settlement. If you roll a 1 when seeing how much gold you receive, then you actually get nothing - it is not an artefact they recognise. You will have to try again in the next Settlement you come to.) DISCARD AFTER SALE
A glass phial of glowing water like this is an ideal tool in the arsenal of many Witch Hunters.

This phial can be thrown as a missile weapon. It causes 1D6 Wounds to any Daemonic, Undead or Chaotic creatures hit. It also causes 1D3 Wounds to any such creatures adjacent to the target, with no modifiers for anything. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Blowing this horn produces an aggressive, guttural sound.

When blown, roll 1D6 for each group of Monsters on the Board (a group being defined as all those Monsters of the same type e.g. Orcs or Skaven). On a score of 4, 5 or 6 that group of Monsters attacks another group of Monsters this Turn (determine randomly) rather than the Warriors. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This Norse horn produces a highly unnerving chord.

When blown, all of the Warrior on the Board get +1 Strength for one Turn. (RPG: In addition, all of the Monsters on the same Board Section who are carrying a Rider become disorientated and suffer -1 Attack for one Turn. This does not affect the Rider, however, who may attack as normal.) USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This jewel-encrusted silver horn has ancient runes engraved upon it.

When the horn is blown, roll 1D6 for every group of Monsters on the Board (a group being defined as all those Monsters of the same type e.g. Orcs or Skaven). On a score of 6, that particular group flees the battle. The Warriors do not receive any gold for Monsters that flee. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This brass horn is said to originate in the Kingdoms of Ind.

When blown, the magical power of this horn allows any Warrior on the Board to enter a hypnotic trance and heal themselves if they choose. The horn may only be used if there are no Monsters on the Board. Entering the trance takes one full Turn, during which the Warrior may do nothing else. At the end of the Turn each Warrior who entered the trance has 1D6 Wounds restored. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This large rusty warhorn shows signs of having been carried in many a battle.

When blown, the mystical power of the horn places a magical shield around each Warrior, cloaking them in a _ near impenetrable cowl of raw magic. The Warriors can only be hit by Monsters scoring a natural 6 To Hit. The effects of the horn last for one Turn. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The air is filled with an eerie howling and incorporeal shadows hurl themselves at the Warrior's foes.

The stone allows a Warrior to summon 1D3 Hounds of Grimnair. The Warrior may choose which Monster each Hound attacks and it doesn't matter if there are any open squares next to target as the Hounds are immaterial beasts. A Hound attacks its target each Turn. It hits on a D6 roll of 6 and causes 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. If a 1 or 2 is rolled for the Hound's Attack or its target is killed, the Hound vanishes. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE

This magical shield was carried by Norse Barbarian Jana the Unforgiving and helped protect her tribe from a rampaging Fire Dragon. Icy crystals protrude from the surface of this shield generating a soft mist around it.
While a Warrior is using this shield they get +1 Toughness (RPG: or they get +2 Toughness against fire-based Attacks). PERMANENT
The Warrior's chill breath fills the room, coalescing around a single Monster and encasing it in a solid tomb of ice. Pick any Monster within the bearer's Line of Sight and roll 1D6.

If the score exceeds the target's Toughness, it is encased ina sarcophagus of ice and may not move or fight. While entombed, target cannot be attacked. Roll 1D6 at the beginning of each Monsters' Phase. If the score is less than or equal to the target's Toughness, it frees itself. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This sturdy jewelled icon twists and turns in the hand of its bearer.
When used, this icon allows the Warrior to add +1 to their roll to Escape from Pinning. USE ONCE PER TURN
This redstone icon is chillingly cold to the touch.

When used, the icon allows the bearer to shove a Monster out of their way. Roll 1D6 and add the Warrior's Strength. If the total is greater than the Monster's Strength it is pushed back into any one of the three squares behind it that is unoccupied. Having pushed their foe back, the Warrior moves into the square that was occupied by the Monster and may now make their normal Attacks. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This small jewelled icon gleams with a cold light. This small icon may be placed upon a shield, melding itself permanently with the surface.
Once per Adventure, this icon allows the Warrior to ignore the Damage from a single blow. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This icon gives the Warrior an impressive sense of direction even in the depths of the darkest dungeon.

If the Warrior bearing this icon flees the Dungeon, they may make two rolls on the Escaping the Dungeon Table and choose the best result. In addition, if the bearer is the leader with Lantern and an Event (RPG: or Travelling Hazard) indicates they are lost, roll 1D6. If the score is a 3, 4, 5 or 6, they may ignore it. PERMANENT
This jeweled icon radiates great destructive energy.

A Warrior may use this icon once per Turn to attempt to destroy one item carried by an adjacent Monster. Roll 1D6. If the score is a 5 or 6, the item is destroyed. The icon lasts until used successfully or until the Warrior rolls a 1 when attemping to use it, both of which render the icon useless and worthless. LASTS UNTIL USED SUCCESSFULLY OR UNTIL A 1 IS ROLLED
This small, decorative urn is covered with magical symbols and runes.
When opened, the urn discharges a fragrant cloud of vapour that acts as a healing potion. Every Warrior on the same Board Section as the urn has 2D6 Wounds restored. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
With a shimmer of light, the Warrior's outline slowly fades until they are completely invisible.

This spell allows the Warrior bearing the scroll to become invisible for one Turn. While invisible the Warrior may automatically Escape from Pinning and carry out any action except attack an opponent: moving, healing, etc. are therefore valid actions, while firing a bow, pushing a Monster into a pit, casting offensive spells, etc. are not. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Warrior bearing the scroll is protected by a magical second skin created by the Iron Skin spell.
When used, the Warrior with the scroll gets +2 Toughness for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Bearing magical sigils this slender silver javelin glitters in the darkness.

This javelin may be thrown as a Strength 4 missile weapon. If the target is successfully hit, and is standing directly in front of, and adjacent to, a wall, roll an extra 1D6 after rolling for Damage. On a score of 5 or 6 the javelin causes normal Damage and pins the target to the wall. A pinned creature may do nothing for the rest of the Turn as it struggles to free itself. The javelin can be retrieved at the end of the Combat. USE ONCE PER COMBAT

Huayi Jong was a sorcerer from Cathay often sought out for his arcane abilities. He invented these cards for his amusement, often demanding that an employer draw one as part the contract for his services.

A Warrior who finds these cards feels compelled to draw one immediately (RPG: and at the beginning of each Adventure). Roll 1D6 to see what effect the magic card has on the Warrior: 1 -1D3 Starting Wounds 2 -1 Strength 3 +1 Weapon Skill 4 +1 Strength 5 +1 Toughness 6 +1 Attack The effect lasts until the end of the Adventure. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
These surprisingly heavy juggling clubs hide a potent secret. There are 1D6 juggling clubs filled with explosive black powder.

Each club can be thrown at any time at an enemy within the Warrior's Line of Sight where it may explode upon impact. Once thrown, roll 1D6 for the Monster and if the score is a 5 or 6, the club remains intact and the Monster immediately throws it back at the Warrior! In that case, roll 1D6 for the Warrior with the same rules listed above. This process continues until either the Warrior or the Monster rolls a 1, 2, 3 or 4, whereupon the club explodes causing 2D6 Wounds. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
Steam rises from the frothy surface of this foul-smelling liquid.

Any Dwarf will recognise it with grudging respect as the product of the Orc Kill Krazy Brew Boyz. No sane Warrior would contemplate drinking the brew, but if they did... There is enough brew for four drinks and each unused drink is worth 125 gold. If a Warrior drinks, roll 1D6: 1 Dead! The Warrior's head explodes in a colourful and noisy fashion. 2 Mindless! The Warrior falls to the floor, fluid leaking from their ears. They ignore all Damage from blows they take for 1D6 Turns, but may do nothing at all. 3 Zonked! The Warrior staggers around in an uncontrolled fashion. Until a 4, 5 or 6 is rolled in the Power Phase, the Warrior may do nothing except wander along blearily behind the others. If attacked, they are at Weapon Skill 1. 4-5 Fortified! The coarse liquor fires up the Warrior's blood, invigorating them whilst stripping the skin off the roof of their mouth. They have 2D6 Wounds restored, but may not talk for the next 1D6 Turns. 6 The Warrior gurgles mindlessly for a moment as the brew reorganizes their internal organs, then a feeling of new-found strength washes over them. They have 1D6 Wounds restored, their Weapon Skill is permanently reduced by -1, but they cause an extra dice of Damage every time they hit. DISCARD WHEN ALL DRUNK

King Esteban of Estalia won a famous battle against the Undead of Lahmia, and attributed his legendary invulnerability to the shield he bore. Its past is shrouded in mystery, but the kings of Tilea have always carried it into battle.
A Warrior bearing this shield gets +1 Toughness. In addition, the bearer may ignore all the effects from any one Attack made against them, whatever its origin. PERMANENT/USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
These unconventional boots glow like molten lava.

While a Warrior is wearing these boots, they gain a special Fire Stomp Attack which causes Wounds as normal with no modifiers for Armour. However, the boots are cumbersome and so the wearer suffers -1 Movement. PERMANENT
The liquid in this small bottle is thin like water and has a floral aroma.

A Warrior who drinks this potion has 1D3 Wounds restored. (RPG: In addition, a Warrior who drink this antidote has 1 point restored to any one Characteristic that had previously been reduced for any reason. This potion cannot restore Wounds that have been permanently reduced.) USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
A crackling sound fills the room as the Warrior raises themselves aloft on a column of coruscating power.

When a Warrior uses this scroll, pick any Warrior on the Board. For one Turn the target levitates. A levitating Warrior can only be attacked with missile weapons or spells. They cannot move, however, and may not fight in hand-to-hand Combat. Their square remains impassable, just as if they were standing in it normally. They may levitate out of a pit. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD

Blazing fire leaps from the Warrior's eyes, striking the Warriors and bathing them in a corona of energy that closes their wounds. Pick any number of Warriors on the Board, including the Warrior bearing the scroll. Roll 1D6 for each Warrior chosen. Each Warrior has that number of wounds restored.
If any two or more of the dice rolled come up with the same number, the spell fails and none of the Warriors have any Wounds restored. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This skull-headed talisman is imbued with the properties of the dreaded Lifestealer spell. The Warrior with the talisman can steal Wounds from a nearby Monster to heal themselves.
The bearer may choose one adjacent Monster who immediately suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. The bearer then has the same number of Wounds restored. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The Lifestone has the power to drain the life energy of others and transfer it to its wearer.

Upon invoking the Lifestone choose any other Warrior on the Board. The bearer now has the same number of Wounds as that Warrior, while they now have the number of Wounds the bearer had. In effect, their Wounds are swapped. The Lifestone may never be used to take a Warrior above their Starting Wounds, which remain as they were. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This phial is filled with a glowing white liquid which lights the way in the darkness just as the Lantern does.

Once this phial is opened, the bearer can use it to explore just as if they carried the Lantern. The power of the phial will be exhausted after one Adventure, or if the Warrior carrying the phial is reduced to zero Wounds. LASTS FOR ONE ADVENTURE
Raw power arcs across the Warrior's fingertips and a bolt of white energy streaks from their eyes toward their opponent.
When a Warrior uses this scroll, any single Monster in the bearer's Line of Sight immediately suffers 2D6 Wounds. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
As soon as this ring is slipped over a finger, it shrinks slightly to fit, and a lightning rune starts to glow coldly on its upper surface.

A Warrior wearing the Lightning Fire Ring can fire a bolt of lightning at any Monster within their Line of Sight. The bolt hits automatically and causes 5D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Armour. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE

This set of tools is designed for unlocking things that were meant to stay secured. Lock tools allow the user to open any lock as long as it is not magical or the rules of the lock specifically state otherwise. To see if the user can open a lock, roll 1D6. On a score of 4, 5 or 6 the lock opens. On a score of 2 or 3 the lock refuses to open at the moment, though the user may try again next Turn.
On a score of 1, the lock tools are snapped off in the lock and are now useless. PERMANENT
Anurandel was a Wood Elf Sorceress, renowned for her peaceful demeanour, sunny disposition and hatred of battle.

She fashioned many items to aid her followers should they be assailed by dark forces. This plain, smooth stone bears an Elf rune. It allows the bearer to re-roll any one dice roll once per Adventure. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Hanging from a fine chain, this palm-sized talisman is said to bring good luck.
A Warrior may use the Lucky Talisman at any time. Any Attack made while using the Lucky Talisman will automatically hit. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The edges of this mace have been expertly sharpened so that it inflicts more damage upon impact This mace causes +1D3 Wounds if the To Hit roll is a natural 6.

A Warrior feels extra aggressive with this mace in their hand. While using this mace, if the Warrior's final regular Attack in a Turn hits and causes at least one Wound (after all deductions), they may make an extra Attack on the Monster again. The Warrior may Keep making an extra Attack against the Monster until they miss or fail to cause a Wound. PERMANENT
These arrows are carved with strange Goblin runes. The arrows are enchanted, but are somewhat unpredictable.

When fired, roll 1D6 below instead of rolling To Hit: 1 The arrow automatically hits one of the Warriors (determined randomly) for 1D6+3 Wounds. 2-5 The arrow automatically hits the Monster and causes 1D6+3 Wounds. 6 The arrow automatically hits the Monster causing 2D6+3 Wounds. There are 6 arrows. Each arrow can only be used once. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
Glowing and vibrating in their hand, the Warrior can see that these are no ordinary beans!

A Warrior may eat a Magic Bean at any time. Roll 1D6 to see what effect the bean has on them for one Turn: 1 +1 To Hit. 2 +1 Movement. 3 +1 Strength. 4 +1 Toughness. 5 +1 Attack. 6 +1D6 Wounds when determining Damage. There are 1D6 beans and each one is worth 50 gold. DISCARD WHEN ALL EATEN
This small glass bottle bears strange inscriptions in some long- forgotten magical language.

When a Warrior drinks the Magic Potion, roll 1D6 to determine what it is: 1 Poison: suffer 2D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. 2 Poison: suffer 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. 3 Rainbow Potion: the Warrior's flesh becomes rainbow striped! 4 Healing Potion: restores 1D6 of the Warrior's Wounds. 5 Healing Potion: restores 2D6 of the Warrior's Wounds. 6 Healing Potion: restores the Warrior to full Wounds. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This blade glows with a yellow light and is slightly warm to the touch.
This fine sword is worth 25 gold. (RPG: This weapon is magical.) PERMANENT
This quiver is made from soft leather and has magical runes branded into its surface.

This fine quiver is worth 25 gold. (RPG: Any arrows or crossbow bolts placed in this quiver immediately become enchanted so they can affect Monsters that can only be hit with magical weapons.) PERMANENT
Spikes protrude from the top of this helm, making it as dangerous as it is protective.

While wearing this helm, a Warrior gets +1 Toughness. In addition, if all of the Warrior's regular Attacks hit this Turn, they receive a special Helm Attack. Roll To Hit as normal. If the helm Attack hits, it causes normal Damage. PERMANENT
This parchment glows with a pale light but appears blank.

However, it may be of value to someone who understands how its magic works. This parchment is worth 50 gold. (RPG: A Warrior may use this map if they wish to find a Special Location in a Settlement without having to roll the dice. They only need to say the name f the place and, if the Settlement would normally include it, the map reveals exactly where it is. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The Warrior sends a sparkling, dancing light down the corridor, drawing their opponents away.

This spell may be cast when Monsters are about to be placed on the Board. Roll 1D6 for each Monster that appears. On a score of 6 that Monster follows the marsh lights away from the Warrior and isn't placed on the Board at all. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This bizarre mask depicts the wicked face of a daemon.

A Warrior who wears this mask takes on the appearance of a daemonic creature. Any Chaos or Daemonic Monster attacking the Warrior suffers -2 To Hit. If another Warrior is adjacent to the mask wearer and rolls a 1 To Hit, however, they have mistakenly hit the mask wearer instead, who suffers Damage as normal! This mask cannot be worn at the same time as a helmet. PERMANENT
A dark cloud begins to swirl around the wearer of this black leather armour.

While wearing this armour a Warrior gets +1 Toughness. Once per Aventure, the armour can produce a black cloud that shrouds the Warrior in darkness. For one Turn Monsters only hit the wearer on a natural To Hit roll of 6. PERMANENT USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Moradrel was a skilled Elf Mage who created many powerful magic items, most of which have been lost or destroyed down the long years.
While wearing these boots a Warrior's Movement Characteristic is 1D6+1 Roll each Turn to see how far the Warrior can move. PERMANENT
This flail seems to have a will of its own, trying to entangle nearby weapons.

If a Warrior using this weapon rolls a natural 6 To Hit, the flail entwines itself around one object the creature struck is holding, yanking it from their grasp. The Monster may do nothing for one Turn as they retrieve it. PERMANENT
One who holds these brightly coloured mushrooms in their hands feels a strange tingling sensation in their fingers. There are 1D3+1 Mushrooms and a Warrior may eat only one each Turn.

If they do, roll 1D6 to see what happens: 1-2 Hallucinogen. Confused, the Warrior attacks one random adjacent model this Turn. 3-4 Panacea. A sense of calm comes over the Warrior and they have 1D6 Wounds restored. 5-6 Stimulant. The Warrior's muscles begin to bulge and they get +3 Strength for the rest of the Turn. DISCARD WHEN ALL EATEN

Any foe whose eyes fall upon this shield sees the haunting vision of their own death. A Warrior carrying this shield gets +1 Toughness. In addition, the bearer may force a Monster to re-roll one of its Attacks against them, but they must accept the outcome of the second roll. USE ONCE PER TURN
Carved into this talisman is the image of a warrior at the heart of a ferocious storm.
When the power of this talisman is invoked, it allows the Barbarian to go Berserk automatically. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This sword is made from the solidified blackness of the void, explaining its ability to destroy any armour it touches.
After a Warrior has made a successful Attack using this sword which actually wounded their opponent, roll 1D6. On a score of 5 or 6 the sword destroys the target's Armour. PERMANENT
This brutal, broad-bladed axe is a powerful weapon and is almost certainly of Dwarf origin.
While using this two-handed axe a Warrior gets +2 Strength (+3 if they are a Dwarf). PERMANENT
Calling on the forces of magic, the Warrior bearing the scroll uses the power to increase the target's strength tremendously.
Pick any Warrior on the Board. That Warrior gets +2 Strength for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This rope is old, worn and has begun to fray.
The rope can be used to escape from a pit. After each use, roll 1D6. On a score of 1, 2, 3 or 4 the rope breaks and must be discarded. PERMANENT

This black orb glistens dully and is far heavier than it looks. This orb contains 1D6 points of Power that the Wizard can use in the same way as his Power Tokens. Place a D6 on top of this card to show the orb's current Power.
Once all of the points are used up it is useless and worthless. It cannot be recharged. The orb is worth 50 gold per unused point of Power. DISCARD WHEN DRAINED
This orb is affixed to a claw-like stand that grips the hand of whoever holds it.
When the power of the orb is invoked, no model on the same Board Section as the bearer may use any ability, magic or item that allows them to fly for one Turn. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The surface of this glittering silver orb, constantly swirls as if in motion and exudes a distinctive magical aura.

This orb contains 1D6 points of Power that the Wizard can use the same way as his Power Tokens. Once all the points are used up it is useless and worthless. (RPG: The Wizard can recharge the Orb at the Font of Power, just like the Wizard's Staff.) USE ONCE THEN RECHARGE
The Grand Theogonist Wilhelm used this sparkling orb in his battle against the Vampire Count Vlad von Carstein.
When the power of the Orb is invoked, no Necromantic Magic may be used by any model on the same Board Section as the bearer for the next 1D6 Turns. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This sword darts forward of its own volition to meet the weapon of an attacker, stopping the blow from hitting home.

Once per Turn a Warrior using this sword may attempt to parry one hand-to-hand Attack that would otherwise hit them. Roll 1D6. On a score of 4, 5 or 6 the attack is magically turned away and has no effect. (RPG: The sword gives the Warrior the Parry 4+ ability.) USE ONCE PER TURN
This long pear! necklace is exquisite and will fetch a fair price when sold.

The necklace is worth 1D6x10 gold. (RPG: The Warrior may try and sell the Pearl Necklace to the General Store. Roll 1D6 and multiply the result by 10 to see how much the trader will offer you for it. However, if you roll a 1, the necklace is fake, and the trader refuses to buy it. You will have to try and sell it at another Settlement.) DISCARD AFTER SALE
The magic sigils on this sword glow brightly whenever an evil spirit draws near.
While a Warrior is using this two-handed sword they get +2 Strength. In addition, when used against Monsters with the Ethereal ability, the wielder gets +1 To Hit and causes Double Damage. PERMANENT
Playing these pipes produces a hauntingly beautiful chord.

When played, the sound from these pipes echo for one Turn. During that time every Warrior on the same Board Section as the player only miss with their regular Attacks if their To Hit roll isa 1. If a Warrior rolls a natural 1 To Hit, however, they blunder their Attack so badly that they hit themselves instead and suffer normal Damage. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Roaring words of power the bearer of the scroll stamps their foot into the ground, cracking the floor with a loud boom.

Place the Pit of Despair marker on any 2 square by 2 square area on the Board. Roll 1D6 for each model standing in the pit area. On a 1 or 2 it falls into the pit and is killed. On a 3, 4, 5 or 6 it scrambles out of the way - put it in an empty square on this or any adjacent Board Section. If there are no empty squares available, the model falls into the pit anyway. Once the pit is in place, no model may enter the squares it covers. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This wickedly sharp and very thin bladed dagger is designed for slipping between armour joints.

A Warrior may use this dagger to make an extra attack by rolling 1D6: 1 The attack misses. 2-4 The attack hits causing 2D6+Strength Wounds with no modifiers for Armour. 5-6 The attacks hits causing 3D6 + Strength Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The pink liquid in this small glass bottle constantly fizzes and bubbles.

At the end of the Adventure, a Warrior may sell this potion to an Alchemist for 1D6x40 gold. (RPG: To sell this potion, the Warrior must first find the Alchemist's Laboratory Special Location in a Settlement!) DISCARD AFTER SALE
This robust, pocket-sized bottle is filled with a sparkling yellow liquid.
A Warrior who drinks this potion gets +1 Attack for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This tiny bottle provides a big effect. The liquid is thick Ike honey and tastes as sweet.
A Warrior who drink this potion may climb walls or over obstacles for one Turn. This potion does not affect the distance that a Warrior may move. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This black liquid has no taste and leaves no trace of moisture on the lips.

A Warrior who drinks this potion will not be attacked and automatically Escapes from Pinning as long as they do not initiate Combat. As soon as the Warrior attacks a target in any way the potion's effects wear off. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This small bottle of sluggish, orange liquid is surprisingly heavy.
A Warrior may drink this potion to ignore the Wounds from a single blow that might otherwise kill them. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The contents of this bottle bubble and pop. This effervescent liquid goes down easily and leaves the Warrior feeling light- headed and light of body!

After drinking this potion the Warrior levitates a few inches above the ground while moving. While airborne the Warrior may pass over chasms, pits, etc unhindered and anyone who attacks them suffers -1 To Hit. The effects last for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The grey surface of this thick necked bottle cannot conceal the golden liquid within.
A Warrior who drinks this potion may fly for one Turn. While airborne they move 1D6+2 squares, can fly over obstacles and may automatically Escape from Pinning. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
A deep red liquid fills this crystal decanter.

A Warrior who drinks this potion gets +1D6 Wounds. These additional Wounds can take the Warrior above their Starting Wounds score. If that is the case, these extra Wounds are temporary. Once they are lost, they are lost for good. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This ornate glass bottle contains a blue coloured liquid with a sweet, fruity smell.

Testing with the smallest sip reveals that it is a potion of healing. There is enough Potion of Healing in this bottle to restore two Warriors to full Wounds. When the first dose is taken, flip this card upside-down; when the second dose is taken, discard it. USE TWICE THEN DISCARD
This fine crystal decanter is full of a glowing orange liquid.

When drunk, this potion shifts the dimensional position of the Warrior, making them _ ethereal and_ insubstantial. They automatically Escape from Pinning, but may not take any offensive action, cast magic or be attacked for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This vial contains a multi-coloured liquid that swirls sluggishly and gives off heady vapours once the seal is broken.
A Warrior who drinks this potion becomes invisible for one Turn. While invisible the Warrior cannot be attacked or cast spells, but may otherwise fight as normal. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
A small magical icon decorates this bottle, inside of which is a fiery yellow liquid.
A Warrior who drinks this potion may make a number of missile attacks equal to their Attack Characteristic this Turn, rather than the standard one shot per Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This tiny glass bottle is full of a bitter purple liquid.
A Warrior who drinks this potion gets +1 To Hit (RPG: +1 To Hit per Battle-level) for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This small bottle contains a cloudy purple substance.
When drunk, this potion restores 1D6 of the Wizard's Power Tokens. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
An intoxicating liquid flows from this bottle, bringing a sense of power and might to whoever drinks it.
A Warrior who drinks this potion gets +1D6 Strength for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This large bottle seems to barely contain the bubbling orange liquid within.
A Warrior may drink this potion to ignore the Wounds from a single blow that might otherwise kill them. In addition, it the Warrior has 2D6 Wounds restored. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This effervescent yellow-orange liquid has a sour taste, but leaves the Warrior feeling powerful.
When a Warrior drinks this potion, their next regular Attack causes maximum Damage if it hits. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Inside this bottle is a lucent orange liquid.

A Warrior who drinks this potion has 1D6 Wounds restored. In addition, the drinker will not suffer any Wounds that occur at the end of the Turn (RPG: the drinker does not suffer any Fatal Damage this Turn). USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
A fiery reddish-orange solution churns in this flask.
A Warrior who drinks this potion flies into a furious rage. They double their regular Attacks and suffer -1 To Hit for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The hue of this coral coloured liquid seems to shift and change with how the bottle is held.

When drunk, this potion allows the Warrior to ignore anything that might affect their respiration, such as water or poison gas. The effects last for 1D6 Turns. There is enough liquid for four drinks. If sold the potion is worth 100 gold for every drink still left in the bottle. DISCARD WHEN ALL DRUNK
The bright red liquid in this bottle swishes about by its own volition.
A Warrior who drinks this potion gets +1 Strength for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Inside this stoppered glass bottle is a glossy blue liquid much thicker than water.
A Warrior who drinks this potion gets +1D6 Movement and may automatically Escape from Pinning. The effects of the potion last for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This shining bottle is full of a thick purple liquid.
A Warrior who drinks this potion gets +1 Toughness for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This effervescent liquid has a salty tang reminiscent of the sea.
A Warrior who drinks this potion may walk on water for one Turn, moving at a rate of (3 x their normal Movement) squares per Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The warm amber liquid in this stoppered glass phial goes down smoothly and leaves a pleasant aftertaste.

A Warrior who drinks this potion gets +2 To Hit and causes +2D6 Wounds when attacking with a missile weapon or in hand-to-hand Combat. The effects of the potion last for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Warrior hears the pleasant sound of coins jingling as they shake this small, leather pouch.
This pouch contains 3 gold pieces. RECORD THEN DISCARD
This mysterious, unusual icon might be worth something to someone.

The casket contains an icon which belongs to the Elders of a far distant village. After the Adventure, the icon may be sold back to the Elders for 1D6x200 gold. (RPG: Obviously the Warrior must first visit a Village to claim their reward!) DISCARD AFTER SALE
This small drawstring bag contains some _ ordinary-looking stones.

When the Warrior looks through the 'stones', roll 1D6 below: 1-2 They are indeed pretty, but ordinary, stones worth nothing. 3-4 Mixed with normal stones are 1D6 gold nuggets wroth 25 gold each. 5-6 Mixed with normal stones are 1D6 gemstones worth 100 gold each. RECORD THEN DISCARD
This shield allows the bearer to anticipate attacks made from a distance.
A Warrior bearing this shield gets +1 Toughness. In addition, all incoming missile Attacks against the bearer suffer -1 To Hit. PERMANENT
This small ring has the image of a shield engraved on its uppermost surface.
A Warrior wearing this ring gets +1 Toughness. PERMANENT
This dusty scroll is inscribed with magic runes and sigils that contain words of power.

This scroll can be used at any time, and causes a 2 square by 2 square area of any one Board Section to cave in. Use the Cave- in marker to mark the area affected. Any Warrior or Monster caught in the area covered by the cave-in suffers 2D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Who created this very unusual, gold-leaf covered, baroque horn is a mystery. This horn can only be used during the Hunting Settlement Event.

When they are alone in the forest, the Warrior may blow the horn and roll 1D6: 1-5 The horn makes an awful noise, like a flatulent ogre. Nothing happens. 6 The horn makes a beautiful, clear sound. A fabulous beast bounds out of the bushes and fawns at the Warrior's feet. They have captured a Quarg! They can sell the Quarg for 1000 gold, but no one will buy the horn off them... ROLEPLAYING GAME ONLY PERMANENT
These bracelets were created by the Elves to complement the fabled Silver Band of Loren.
A Warrior wearing these bracelets gets +1 Attack when using a missile weapon. PERMANENT
This mighty sword is sharper than a razor. Its keen blade glistens in the light of the lantern, and even in the shadows it shimmers with an eerie inner light.
While a Warrior is using this sword they get +2 Strength. PERMANENT
As the bearer of this scroll chants the words of this spell, a shimmering mirror of power surrounds the target, protecting them from harm.

Pick any Warrior on the Board. Each time they are physically struck by a blow this Turn, roll 1D6. On a score of 6 all the Damage from the blow is rebounded onto whoever caused it. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This holy blade is an artefact of great religious significance within the Empire, and was reputed to have been used by the realm's mightiest heroes in numerous battles.

While a Warrior is using this sword they get +2 To Hit. (RPG: If the To Hit roll is a natural 1, however, the Warrior misses and is so off-balance that they lose any remaining Attacks they have this Turn.) PERMANENT
Magic runes of warding are emblazoned upon this otherwise smooth helm.

The wearer of this helm gets +1 Toughness. In addition, each time the wearer is hit by a missile attack, roll 1D6. If the score is a 6, the attack ricochets off and hits a random Monster within the wearer's Line of Sight instead. But if the result is a 1, the attacks pings off and hits a random Warrior within the wearer's Line of Sight instead! PERMANENT
Anurandel was a Wood Elf Sorceress, renowned for her peaceful demeanour, sunny disposition and hatred of battle. She fashioned many items to aid her followers should they be assailed by dark forces.

If the bearer of the ring takes no offensive action this Turn, the ring will restore 1D6 Wounds at the end of the turn. If the score is a 1 or 2 the ring's power is drained for the rest of the Adventure. PERMANENT

This ring crackles with power and feels unusually heavy for its size. The Ring of Dadaan allows a Warrior to cast a spell. Take one of the Wizard's Starting Spell Cards at random: this is the spell that is stored in the ring.
The bearer of the ring may cast the stored spell automatically at any time. After use, the ring is drained of all magic and becomes worthless. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Slipping this ring on, the wearer fades from sight.

While wearing this ring, a Warrior is invisible and cannot be attacked, although they may fight as normal. The ring contains enough power to Keep the bearer invisible for one Turn. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Within the central gemstone of this silver, jewel-encrusted ring, a tiny flame burns brightly.

The ring has the power to heal grievous wounds. It may only be used to heal another Warrior (not the bearer) if they are on zero Wounds. To use the ring, roll 1D6. On a roll of 1 the flame flickers and dies, and has no effect. On any other score the Warrior is restored to full Wounds. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This simple band of stone slips easily onto a Warrior's finger, contracting until it is a perfect fit.

While wearing this ring one of the Warrior's Characteristics is increased by +1. The Warrior may choose to increase Movement, Weapon Skill, Ballistic Skill, Strength, Toughness, Initiative, Attacks, Luck, Willpower or Escape Pinning. The Warrior must choose which Characteristic is affected the first time they put on the ring. PERMANENT
This silver ring is inset with tiny emeralds in the shape of leaves.

When drawing a Warrior Counter to determine who the target is for a group of Monsters who all attack a single Warrior (RPG: Monsters who have the Gang Up ability), the first time the bearer's counter is pulled out they may put it back and draw again. If it comes up a second time, they are attacked as normal. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This ring has a large gem set in its surface that, in times of danger, warns of what is to come.

A Warrior wearing this ring may swap an Event Card that has just been drawn for another. Shuffle ALL the other Event Cards together (including any that have already been used) to make a new Event Deck. Take the top card and carry on as usual. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This baton is actually a powerful rod that can give or take life. The Warrior wielding this rod may choose up to 3 targets and roll 3D6.

The result is the number of points the Warrior may use to heal the targets as they see fit. But note that the points of healing from a single dice cannot be divided and must be spent on a single Warrior, and all targets must be healed. If two of the dice come up a 1, however, all of the targets suffer 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. When the rod is found it contains 1D6 charges. Each use drains one charge. The rod is worth 75 gold per unused charge. DISCARD WHEN DRAINED
This stone of Dwarf construction is inscribed with a glowing rune.

When pressed into contact with any bladed weapon, the runestone burns a Rune of Death into the blade and then vanishes. The Rune of Death itself is permanent. For one Attack per Adventure, this weapon causes +2D6 Wounds. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This stone of Dwarf construction is inscribed with a glowing rune.

When pressed into contact with any bladed weapon, the runestone burns a Rune of Destruction into the blade and then vanishes. The Rune of Destruction itself is permanent. Once per Adventure, the wielder of this weapon can double their regular Attacks for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This stone of Dwarf construction is inscribed with a glowing rune.

When pressed into contact with any bladed weapon, the runestone burns a Rune of Fortitude into the blade and then vanishes. The Rune of Fortitude itself is permanent. At the beginning of each Adventure the wielder of this weapon gets +1D6 extra Starting Wounds. These Wounds are temporary and once they are lost, they are lost for the rest of the Adventure. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This stone of Dwarf construction is inscribed with a glowing rune.

When pressed into contact with any bladed weapon, the runestone burns a Rune of Fury into the blade and then vanishes. The Rue of Fury itself is permanent. While wielding this weapon a Warrior gains +1 Attack. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This stone of Dwarf construction is inscribed with a glowing rune.

When pressed into contact with any bladed weapon, the runestone burns a Rune of Magic into the blade and then vanishes. The Rune of Magic itself is permanent. The rune glows dully, and Keeps the blade sharp. From now on, this weapon is treated as being magical. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This stone of Dwarf construction is inscribed with a glowing rune.

When pressed into contact with any bladed weapon, the runestone burns a Rune of Prowess into the blade and then vanishes. The Rune of Prowess itself is permanent. While wielding this weapon the Warrior gets +1 To Hit. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This stone of Dwarf construction is inscribed with a glowing rune.

When pressed into contact with any bladed weapon, the runestone burns a Rune of Slaying into the blade and then vanishes. The Rune of Slaying itself is permanent. For one Attack per Adventure this weapon will now inflict Double Damage when it strikes. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This stone of Dwarf construction is inscribed with a glowing rune.

When pressed into contact with any bladed weapon, the runestone burns a Rune of Smashing into the blade and then vanishes. The Rune of Smashing itself is permanent. This weapon now ignores 1 point of Armour when determining Damage. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This stone of Dwarf construction is inscribed with a glowing rune.

When pressed into contact with any bladed weapon, the runestone burns a Rune of Sure Striking into the blade and then vanishes. The Rune of Sure Striking itself is permanent. When rolling more than one Damage Dice, the wielder of this weapon may re-roll the Damage Dice that scores the lowest. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Although this sack is tattered, the gold coins within it still glisten.
The sack contains 1D6x50 gold. RECORD THEN DISCARD
Inside a stone container is a thick red paste.

If a Warrior applies this salve to themselves or one of their companions, it immediately restores 1D3 of the recipient's Wounds. If a natural 1 is rolled when determining how many Wounds are restored, the effects of the salve are complete. Otherwise, the salve will continue restoring 1D3 Wounds at the beginning on the next Turn. This process continues until a natural 1 is rolled when determining how many Wounds are restored. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This stoppered jade-stone phial contains a glittering bronze salve.
A Warrior who uses this salve gets +1 Strength for 1D6 Turns. There is only enough salve in the phial for one application. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Mad Sorcerer of Araby, Abdul Alhazred, created this magical timepiece so that he could steal a powerful artefact from the Palace of the Wizard Caliph.

When this hourglass is turned over, time stops. Every model on the Board, except the bearer of the hourglass, is frozen in time. The bearer may automatically Escape from Pinning and can do anything (move, heal, etc.) but may not take any offensive action. At the beginning of each Turn thereafter, the bearer must roll 1D6. If the score is a 1, 2 or 3, the sands have reached the bottom and time begins again. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This satchel has the mark of the Temple Hospice of Shallya in

Altdorf and is usually carried by travelling priests. The Satchel contains 1D6 Bandages. Each bandage restores 3 Wounds. (RPG: At the end of each Adventure, any remaining Bandages in the satchel vanish. However, at the start of the next Adventure, the satchel will have magically replenished itself, and will contain 1D6 Bandages again!) 1D6 BANDAGES PER ADVENTURE
Alikahn the Keeper was an expert beast handler from Araby. This sceptre helped him control the may wild creatures that he kept in his menagerie.

A Warrior who wields this sceptre as a weapon gets +1 To Hit with it. (RPG: In addition, if a Warrior wields the sceptre in Combat, Monsters on the same Board Section as the bearer are not subject to the Frenzy or Hate abilities.) PERMANENT
This ram-headed scepter feels heavy in the hand when swung against certain foes.
The sceptre may be wielded like a weapon, and causes Double Damage against any Chaos creatures. PERMANENT

When unrolled, words of power glow dully on this ancient parchment. The spell on this scroll gives the bearer the appearance of a ferocious monster. The scroll may be read at any time, causing any Monsters to be so terrified of the affected Warrior that they will suffer -2 To Hit against them. The spell lasts until a 1 or 2 is rolled in the Power Phase. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This mysterious token has a large, laughing skull in its center.

The power of this curio may be used when a Counter is drawn randomly (such as a Warrior Counter, Catacombs Counter, Creep Counter, etc.) The bearer of this token may ignore the result and draw another one instead. The first counter drawn is then mixed back in with the others, as if it had never been drawn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This shield is of simple yet exquisite craftsmanship. It seems to be made of a substance stronger than steel, yet weighs little more than a sheet of parchment.
