A Warrior bearing this shield gets +1 Toughness. In addition, they may ignore the Damage from any two blows. The first time the shield is used, flip this card upside-down; when it is used a second time, discard it. USE TWICE THEN DISCARD
Inside a long abandoned crate the Warriors discover elegant silks from the distant land of Cathay.
There are 1D3 pieces of beautiful silk and each one will fetch 25 gold. (RPG: If sold in a Town, each piece is worth 50 gold. If sold ina City, each piece is worth 100 gold. DISCARD AFTER SALE
This ring was created by the Elves to complement the fabled Rapid Fire Bracelets.
A Warrior wearing this ring gets +1 Ballistic Skill. PERMANENT
These glowing silver cubes were fashioned from the Ogham Stones on the misty island of Albion.

A Warrior may toss these cubes down at the feet of a single Monster during Combat by choosing as many D6 as they wish and rolling them all at the same time. For every dice that comes up a 6, the Monster suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. But for every dice that comes up a 1, the Warrior suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. If this reduces the Warrior to zero Wounds, they are dead and cannot be revived. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This dagger is made of pure silver and therefore highly sought after by Witch Hunters.

A Warrior using this dagger gets +1 To Hit. The dagger is Strength 2 and can be used in hand-to-hand Combat or as a missile weapon. Against Undead and Chaos creatures the dagger is considered magical and causes Double Damage if the To Hit roll is a 5 or 6. If thrown, it may be retrieved at the end of the Combat. PERMANENT
Doznes of magical runes symbolising freedom, capability and advantage have been etched onto this blade.
While wielding this sword a Warrior gets +3 Initiative. In addition, the wielder may add +1 to their roll to Escape from Pinning. PERMANENT
The Silver Stone imbues a weapon it touches with magical powers.

The stone may be used once to permanently enchant a single weapon. The Silver Stone vanishes, but the affected weapon is now magical, and causes +1D6 Wounds on a To Hit roll of 6. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The head of these arrows are made from pure silver.
These arrows cause +1 Wound. (RPG: If used against a Werewolf, these arrows cause Double Damage instead.) There are 1D3 arrows and each arrow may be used only once. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
These magical arrows were enchanted long ago by an Elven mage.
A Warrior using these arrows gets +1 To Hit and cause +1D3 Wounds when attacking Monsters with the F/y ability. There are 2D6 arrows and each may be used only once. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
Murmuring quietly, the bearer of the scroll sends their opponents into an enchanted sleep.

The bearer may target any Monster on the same Board Section by rolling 4D6. If the total score is more than the target's Starting Wounds it falls asleep for 1 Turn. The dice may be split between multiple targets. The bearer must declare how the dice are being split before rolling. If split, the total of the combined dice for each target must be greater than its Starting Wounds for the spell to work on that Monster. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Soulstone captures the departing soul of the bearer, should they be slain. Someone with the correct knowledge can then restore the soul to their body, resurrecting the unfortunate person.

If the Warrior with the Soulstone is killed, by whatever means, they can be resurrected once the Adventure is over. They lose any Treasure and gold they have accumulated during that Adventure, and are unchanged from the Warrior they were when they set off on the Adventure. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This hefty-looking spear has a wickedly sharp head.

In addition to any other attacks they may have, a Warrior may throw this spear as a missile weapon at any time, at a target on the same Board Section as the wielder. To throw the spear, roll 1D6. On a 3, 4, 5 or 6 the Warrior has hit the target and causes 3D6 Wounds. They spear may be retrieved at the end of the Combat. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Breathing deeply, the Warrior uses the power of the scroll to speed up their movement.
A Warrior who uses this scroll has their Movement doubled for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD

Any Wizard recognizes this potent ring as being capable of storing magical knowledge. Take one Starting Spell Card at random: this is the spell that is stored in the ring. The bearer of the ring may cast the stored spell automatically at any time. After use, the ring is drained and the Spell Card must be discarded. (RPG: If it is drained, the ring will slowly recharge itself. At the start of the next Adventure take a new Starting Spell Card to represent the new spell that is stored in the ring.) USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Araby, scorched land of sand and blazing sun, is home to some of the most learned men in the world.
This earthenware pot is full of rich spices, and their pungent tang fills the air. The spices are worth 1D3x100 gold. DISCARD AFTER SALE
This bottle is filled with a sparkling purple solution.

A Warrior may throw this bottle as a missile weapon at any time with a Range of 4 squares. It hits automatically, shattering upon impact. Roll 1D6. If the score is equal to or greater than the target's Movement Characteristic, the target is truck in a pool of gooey paste reminiscent of a spider's web. They cannot move or perform any offensive actions for one Turn, and any Attacks made against them get +1 To Hit. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This fine weapon seems to magically reshape to fit into the wielder's hand perfectly.
A Warrior wielding this mace causes +1 Wound (RPG: +1 Wound per Battle-level) if their To Hit roll is a 5 or 6. PERMANENT

This large cone-shaped device vibrates with energy. This metal cone may be placed on the ground during Combat where it spins out of control with an extraordinary amount of force. Roll 1D6 for each model on the Board Section Cincluding any Warrior).

If the score is a 6 when rolling for a Monster, it is Knocked down and spends the next Monsters' Phase getting back up. Any Warrior attacking a fallen Monster gets +2 To Hit. If the score is a 1 when rolling for a Warrior, they are Knocked down, must spend one full Turn getting up and Monsters attacking them get +2 To Hit. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Ghamaluk was a grand vizier of Araby and created a series of stone icons specifically to combat the Undead hordes amassed against him.

Many of these icons have passed into the hands of the famous Witch Hunter Gunter Grafftag of Sylvania. This icon may be used to restore the bearer back to full Wounds. It fails to work while the bearer is on zero Wounds. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This stone gives great confidence to the bearer, such that fear seems but a dream.
This valuable semi-precious stone is worth 250 gold. (RPG: The bearer gets +1 to all psychology based tests such as Fear and Terror.) PERMANENT
This small, plain stone hangs from a delicate copper chain.

The item may be used to transmute a single Monster into stone, killing it. To determine whether the stone works roll 1D6+1 (RPG: 1D6 + the Warrior's Battle-level). If the total is greater than the target Monster's current Wounds, it becomes a statue, otherwise the spell fails. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Dwarf stonebread is exactly what its name suggests - bread with the consistency of stone.

Only a Dwarf would ever consider eating Stonebread, and they take a gruff satisfaction in the fact that only they can eat the stuff. Eating Stonebread is obviously no quick task. If a Dwarf does nothing for a Turn, except chew their way through a piece of Stonebread, at the end of the Warriors' Phase they have 1D6 Wounds restored. There are 1D6 pieces. DISCARD AFTER ALL EATEN
Sparks fly from this warhammer each time it strikes.
If a Warrior wielding this weapon rolls a natural 6 To Hit, in addition to normal Damage, the target is stunned and suffers - 1 Attack for one Turn. PERMANENT
The scroll bears the words for the Strength spell. When cast, the targeted Warrior feels renewed energy surging through their aching muscles.
When the scroll is used, pick any Warrior on the Board. That Warrior gets +1 Strength for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This sizeable wooden mallet is the kind used in carnivals to test one's strength.

This magical two-handed mallet causes +1D6 Wounds (or Double Damage if the To Hit roll is a natural 6) and ignores non- Magical Armour. The mallet is deliberately weighted poorly, however, so each time a Warrior attemps to swing it, they must roll 1D6 and add their Strength. If the total is 7 or more, they are able to use it normally. If the total is 6 or less, they suffer -2 To Hit. PERMANENT
This amulet radiates warmth and comfort to the wearer.

A Warrior wearing this amulet gets +2 Starting Wounds. (RPG: In addition, each Turn that there is a Vampire on the same Board Section as the wearer, roll 1D6. If the score is a 4, 5 or 6, the Vampire may not use their special Vampire Ability this Turn.) PERMANENT

While holding this sword, the bearer feels light and unencumbered by their belongings. A Warrior wielding this sword gets +2 to any dice rolls when trying to escape spider webs, goblin nets or any other type of physical restraints. In addition, they may ignore any terrain effects or Armour penalties that would reduce their Movement. PERMANENT
This sword is imbued with the strength of an Ogre, and any blow struck with it delivers that power to the target.
While a Warrior is using this sword they get +1 Strength. PERMANENT
This sword throws a mantle of power around its wielder, protecting them from their enemies.
While a Warrior is using this sword they get +1 Toughness. PERMANENT
A Warrior wielding this weapon feels their skin hardening into a stone-like material, which remains as flexible as normal flesh. While a Warrior is using this sword they get +2 Toughness. PERMANENT
Instead of wielding one sword, a great many blades suddenly start to whirl and slash the air before the Warrior bearing the scroll.
When used, the Warrior bearing the scroll gets +2 Attacks. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Anurandel was a Wood Elf Sorceress of Loren, renowned for her peaceful demeanour, sunny disposition and hatred of battle.

She fashioned many items to aid her followers should they be assailed by dark forces. The tablet has the power to completely destroy a single item carried by an enemy. Roll 1D6. If the score is a 5 or 6, the item may no longer be used. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This talisman protected a great Priest King against the decaying magic of the evil Liche Lord Nagash himself. This talisman allows the Warrior to counter Necromantic Magic.

Each time a Necromantic spell is cast against the bearer of the amulet, roll 1D6. On a score of 5 or 6 the spell has no effect on them. (RPG: the amulet gives the bearer the Magic Resistance 5+ ability against Necromantic spells). PERMANENT
The gemstone in the center of this talisman glows like a full moon obscured by dark clouds.

When the power of this talisman is invoked, all Monsters on the Board with the Fly ability only hit the Warriors on a natural To Hit roll of 6, while any Warrior attacking those Monsters only misses on a natural To Hit roll of 1. The effects of the talisman last for one Turn. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This magical artefactsucks all of the light from the surrounding area, creating an aura of darkness.

A Warrior bearing the Talon of Death may use it once they have finished moving to cause 1D6 Wounds on every adjacent Monster, with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. Roll for each Monster separately. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Although covered in verdigris, this metal tankard is obviously of fine craftsmanship.
This is a Dwarf tankard, specially designed for the drinking of Bugman's beer. Tankards like this are much valued by Dwarfs. If a Dwarf finds this tankard, they will never sell it! DISCARD AFTER SALE
This beautiful tapestry is intricately patterned and most likely hails from a faraway land such as Ind or Cathay.
Such tapestries are highly sought after by affluent nobles. It is worth 1D6x50 gold. DISCARD AFTER SALE
Grown in the rich and fertile fields of the majestic island empire, these tea leaves are highly sought after for their unique flavour.
There are 1D6 small bundles of leaves and each is worth 10 gold. (RPG: If sold in a Town, each bundle is worth 20 gold. If sold in a City, each bundle is worth 30 gold.) DISCARD AFTER SALE
This is an extremely well balanced throwing knife that seems to leap from the thrower's hand.

A Warrior may throw the knife at any Monster in their Line of Sight that is not in an adjacent square to them. The knife hits on a roll of 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6. It causes 2D6+Strength Wounds with no modifiers for Toughness and Armour. The knife may be retrieved at the end of the Combat. USE ONCE PER COMBAT
This polished bronze ring expands and contracts to fit around its target.

This ring may be thrown at any time as a missile weapon with a Range of 3 squares. On a successfully hit, the ring drops over one of the Monster's limbs and constricts. The Monster may not cast spells and suffers -2 To Hit. At the beginning of each subsequent Turn, roll 1D6. If the score is a 6, the ring remains in place and the target suffers the penalties above. On any other score, the Monster manages to free itself. The ring may be retrieved at the end of the Combat. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE

When this ring is put on, it freezes like ice and bursts into a cold blue flame, yet still feels comfortable to wear. The Time Freeze Ring may be used at any time, giving the Warriors an additional Warriors' Phase straight away. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Strands of energy swim serenely throughout this small blue stone.
If the bearer does nothing, including move, for one Turn, and there are no Monsters on the Board, they have 1 Wound restored. PERMANENT
The Warrior mumbles a few words and then disappears in a mist of yellow smoke.

A Warrior may use this scroll at any time by rolling 1D6. If the result is a 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6, the Warrior may immediately move from the space they are in to any unoccupied space on the Board that has already been explored. If the score is a 1, the Warrior disappears and is never seen again. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This tunic made from bear and wolf hides has been enchanted by a Norse shaman.
While a Warrior is wearing this tunic, they get +1 Stregnth. If the tunic is worn in addition to armour, the wearer suffers -1 Movement and -1 To Hit. PERMANENT
This sealed silver pot is full of a thick and foul smelling unguent.

A weapon coated with this unguent causes +1 Wound (RPG: +1 Wound per Battle-level) to any Undead it hits. There is enough unguent to coat one weapon and the effects last for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Warrior temporarily leaves the physical universe for the cold depths of the void, where they can recover their strength.

When used, this scroll allows the bearer to disappear from the Board. After one whole Turn, roll 1D6. If the result is a 2, 3, 4,5 or 6, they reappear anywhere on the same Board Section with all of their Wounds restored. If the result is a 1, however, the Warrior disappears in the void and must be removed from the game. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD

This cool blue steel blade flickers as if alive, creating a deadly shimmering blur through which none may pass. In a Corridor or Doorway, no Monster may pass the Warrior while they use this sword, whether they are flying Monsters or even Monsters who are never Pinned.
The bright, magical light of this wand radiates an aura of protection.
The bearer of the wand can use it to explore just as if they carried the Lantern. In addition, a Warrior carrying this wand gets +1 Toughness. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Molten lava spews from this warhammer each time it finds its mark.

This two-handed warhammer causes Double Damage to a target if the To Hit roll is a natural 6, and all adjacent models to the target (friend or foe) suffer 1 Wound with no modifiers for anything. However, if the wielder rolls a natural 1 To Hit, they are splashed with magma and suffer 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. PERMANENT
This card appears on the dungeon treasure table, but the scan did not preserve its description text clearly enough to recover it reliably.
The full rules text could not be recovered from the source scan.
The Warrior calls on the powers of the scroll to guide their hand as they move to strike down their enemies.
When used, the Warrior bearing the scroll gets +2 Weapon Skill for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Wizard-warrior Oscurus the Intrepid carried this staff into the darkest dungeons of the Old World.

While a Warrior is using this staff, they get +1 Attack. Each time the wielder rolls a natural 6 To Hit with the staff the target is flung into the air, and crashes down into an unoccupied square (determined at random) on the same Board Section. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This cowl has been imbued with a mysterious energy during the rituals of a Norse shaman.
A Warrior wearing this cowl gets +1 Toughness (RPG: or +2 Toughness against any cold-based attacks). This cowl cannot be worn at the same time as a helmet. PERMANENT

The scroll creates a dark portal that hypnotically attracts Monsters. The portal is created in any empty square within the bearer's Line of Sight. Roll 1D6 for each Monster adjacent to the portal. On a score of 1, 2, 3 or 4 that Monster stands its ground and may ignore the portal. On a roll of 5 or 6 the Monster is hypnotically attracted to the portal, steps through it and vanishes.
Once you have rolled for each adjacent Monster, the portal closes with a loud crack. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The kingdom of Bretonnia is a lordly realm of knights and castles, barons and chivalry.

The Bretonnians are second only to the Elves of Ulthuan when it comes to the creation of fine wines, and their best vintages fetch a high price throughout the world. There are 1D6 unopened bottles of 100-year old wine, a spectacular find indeed! Each bottle is worth 100 gold. DISCARD AFTER SALE
Leathery wings sprout from the Warrior's back, lifting them into the air.

This scroll allows the Warrior bearing it to fly for one Turn. While airborne the Warrior may not make any attacks, and may not be attacked in hand-to-hand Combat. While in the air a Warrior moves normally and may ignore any ground-based obstacles such as chasms or pits. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This ornate staff radiates with power.

If the Wizard wields the staff like a weapon, he gets +1 Toughness but suffers -1 To Hit. The Wizard may use the staff to re-roll the Power Phase roll once Per Adventure. The Wizard's Staff contains 1 point of Power, which the Wizard can use in the same way as his Power Tokens. (RPG: The Wizard may recharge the staff by visiting the Font of Power.) PERMANENT USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The Kingdom of Kislev is famous for its history of horse warriors.
This masterfully wood-carved horse statue, decorated with tiny gemstones, hails from the war-driven nation of Kislev, and is worth 1D6x25 gold. DISCARD AFTER SALE
This magic paint is imbued with the power of Orion, giving an Elf even more agility and nimbleness during battle.

If an Elf smears this paint on their arms and face, any Monsters attacking them suffer -1 To Hit in hand-to-hand Combat and -2 To Hit with a missile weapon. Once applied, the paint lasts for one Combat. There is enough paint for 1D3 applications. Each unused application is worth 100 gold. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
This staff was made from the bones of a wyvern and the essence of the creature is said to still reside within.
A Warrior who wields this two-handed weapon gets +2 Toughness and causes +1 Wound but suffers -1 To Hit. PERMANENT

Zhen was a warrior from Cathay who played a significant role in expelling the Hobgoblin invaders.. The first Attack each Turn made against the wearer of this gauntlet suffers -1 To Hit. For one Turn per Adventure all Attacks against the wearer only hit on a natural 5 or 6 To Hit.
If a Warrior wears both gauntlets, the first attacks suffers -2 To Hit and for one Turn per Adventure all Attacks made against them only hit on a natural 6. PERMANENT USE ONE TURN PER ADVENTURE

Zhen was a warrior from Cathay who played a significant role in expelling the Hobgoblin invaders... The first Attack each Turn made against the wearer of this gauntlet suffers -1 To Hit. For one Turn per Adventure all Attacks against the wearer only hit on a natural 5 or 6 To Hit.
If a Warrior wears both gauntlets, the first attacks suffers -2 To Hit and for one Turn per Adventure all Attacks made against them only hit on a natural 6. PERMANENT USE ONE TURN PER ADVENTURE
This amulet glows with a reddish light and has the power to transmute the energy of its wearer.

A Warrior wearing this amulet gets +2 Toughness and may ignore any spell cast against them on a D6 roll of 6 (RPG: they get +1 Willpower and the Magic Resistance 6+ ability). To achieve this, the amulet saps the wearer's strength and agility. While wearing the amulet the Warrior suffers -1 Strength, -1 To Hit and -2 Initiative. Once donned, this amulet cannot be removed. PERMANENT
The soft, magical glow from this amulet betrays the fact that it is anything but ordinary. This amulet is worth 500 gold.

If used by a Witch Hunter, the amulet has 1D6 charges and each charge confers the Magic Resistance ability for one Turn. When the amulet is activated roll 1D6 for each spell cast against the bearer. On a score of 4, 5 or 6, the spell has no effect. (RPG: The amulet can be recharged (or ruined) at the Witch Hunters' Guild like other amulets the Witch Hunter has, but it will never be one that appears on the altar.) LASTS UNTIL DESTROYED
This strange ornate box has no apparent lid.

A Warrior may attempt to open this box during Combat by rolling 1D6. If the score is a 1, the box explodes and the Warrior suffers 3D6 Wounds. If the score is a 2 or 3, the box does not open but the Warrior may try again next Turn. If the score is a 4, 5 or 6, the box opens and beams of light shoot out toward the Warrior's foes. Every Monster in the Warrior's Line of Sight suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. Roll for each Monster separately. At the beginning of the next Adventure, the box mysteriously closes and can be used again. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE LASTS UNTIL EXPLODES
The glow from this deep red armour lights up the area.
This armour makes the wearer harder to hit: they get +4 Toughness and any Monster attacking them suffers -1 To Hit unless using a Magic Weapon. PERMANENT
This armour looks pitted and dull, but it glows with a dim radiance that betrays its true nature.

A Warrior wearing this armour gets +3 Toughness. However, once worn it fuses to the Warrior's flesh and cannot be removed. Unfortunately, given its weight, it cannot be carried. It must be donned immediately or left where it is found. PERMANENT

This matt black suit of full armour has dull bronze battle runes engraved on the breastplate, arms and helm. It is said to be endowed with a life of its own, striking at the enemy no matter what its wearer intends.

A Warrior wearing this suit gets the benefits of Heavy Armour and a Warhelm (+5 Toughness, -1 Movement). Every time an enemy wounds the Warrior the armour retaliates and forces its wearer to strike back. Make a single Attack against the enemy that just hit them. Note that, if successful, this attack does not cause a Death-blow. PERMANENT
Turiss was a fearsome Warrior who had this armour enchanted to protect him against Necromantic Magic.

A Warrior wearing this armour gets +2 Toughness. In addition, roll 1D6 any time a Necromantic spell is cast against the wearer. If the score is a 4, 5 or 6, the spell has no effect. If the score is a 1, however, the armour is destroyed and must be discarded. LASTS UNTIL DESTROYED
These arrows were cut from the greatest trees in the forest of Loren and carefully crafted so that their flight is the truest of all.
A Warrior using these arrows gets +2 To Hit. In addition, they ignore 1 point of Armour and cause +1 Wound. There are 3D6 arrows and each arrow may only be used once. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
This axe can overpower even the most steadfast Warrior.

A Warrior wielding this axe gets +1 Attack and +1 Strength. If Monsters are on the Board at the start of the Warriors' Phase, roll 1D6 to see how the Warrior reacts this Turn: 1 Insane! They attack the nearest Warrior, ignoring Pinning, and suffer -1 To Hit. Roll again next Turn. 2-5 Fury! They attack the nearest Monster but suffer -1 To Hit. Roll again next Turn adding +1 to the roll. 6 Control! The Warrior may fight as normal and need not roll again this Combat. PERMANENT

This magical axe was forged many centuries ago by the Priest Kings of the ancient realm of Khemnri. It is a mighty weapon with a pure white blade. When their kingdom fell, the axe was lost, presumed destroyed.

When using this Axe, the wilder may roll an extra dice for Damage and discard the lowest score. They then add their Strength to the score as normal. In addition, when used against Undead, the wielder gets +1 To Hit. (RPG: The abilities of this axe cannot be added to in any way, such as runes etc.) PERMANENT
This axe was once wielded by the Dwarf Giant Slayer Umgrul

Grunnson at the final battle of Karak Azgal. For one Attack this axe automatically hits its target. Roll1D6 to determine the axe's Damage. If the score is a 1, the axe causes 1D6+Strength Wounds. If the score is a 2, the axe causes 2D6+Strength Wounds, and so on. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE

Upon donning these bracelets the Warrior feels a surge of tremendous power coursing through their veins. While a Warrior wears these bands, they get +1 Strength and +1 Toughness. In addition, once per Adventure one of the Warrior's regular Attacks causes Double Damage. The bands cannot be worn with any armour that gives the wearer more than +2 Toughness (RPG: until the wearer is Battel-level 4 or higher). PERMANENT
As soon as it is drawn in the presence of its hated foe this blade snarls and growls, eager to spill blood.

Draw an Event Card until it reveals Monsters to determine what sort of Monsters the sword hates. (RPG: Roll on the Monster Table that is the same level as the Warrior's Battle-level). The sword causes Double Damage against all Monsters of that race. df you roll Skaven Assassins, for instance, the Bane Sword affects all Skaven.) PERMANENT
Michel d'un Doigt (one finger) was a powerful Bretonnian

Wizard. He laboured long to create a series of enchanted artefacts to be used by the chivalric knights of that realm. Unfortunately, the essential ingredient in the creation of each was powdered bone from a human finger. Michel created nine in all... This is the fabled Battle Stone of the Marquis. Any one of the Warrior's Attacks (but not a Death-blow) automatically hits the target. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This thick leather belt belonged to the Dwarf Gagron the Giantslayer who lived to a surprisingly ripe old age.

Each time the wearer of this belt is reduced to zero Wounds, roll 1D6 at the end of the Turn (as long as the Warrior has received no other form of healing during the Turn). On a score of 3, 4 or 5 the belt restores 1D6 Wounds. On a score of 6 the belt restores 2D6 Wounds. Once donned, the Belt of Gagron cannot be removed. PERMANENT
Ass soon as it is drawn this blade strikes with incredible speed.
While wielding this sword a Warrior gets +2 Attacks. PERMANENT
This blade Is superbly balanced and moves in a swift golden arc with almost no effort on the wielder's part, slicing through the enemy ranks with ease.
While wielding this sword a Warrior gets +3 Attacks. PERMANENT

This pistol delivers a burning blast of shot can perforate even the most durable armour. This pistol is a missile weapon with Strength 6 and a Range of 6. It takes one Turn to reload after it has been fired.
Any shot fire from this pistol becomes so hot that it ignores all non-masgical Armour. There is enough gunpowder and shot to last one Adventure. PERMANENT
A lustrous blood-red stone is mounted in the center of this ring.

Each time the wearer of this ring suffers Wounds, the ring absorbs one of the Wounds instead of the wearer. The ring can store a total number of Wounds equal to the wearer's Starting Wounds score. If there are no Monsters on the Board, the wearer can use as many of the stored Wounds as they would like to heal themselves. Once the ring is put on, it may not be removed. PERMANENT

This ominous-looking longsword perpetually drips blood onto the floor. After any other attacks the Warrior using this sword makes each Turn, roll 1D6 for each model (Warriors as well as Monsters) in a square adjacent to them.
On a score of 6, the sword lashes out and strikes the target of its own volition, causing Damage as normal. PERMANENT

The pages of this aged book rustle with a life of their own. This book contains spells that a Wizard may cast. Take any Spell Cards not currently in play to represent the spells contained within the book. (RPG: Roll 4D6. These dice are used to buy spells for the book in the same way the Wizard gains spells when he goes up a Battle-level.)
Each spell may be cast once automatically at no Power cost. Once a spell has been cast that page of the book crumbles to dust. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
This ancient book whispers the name of the Warrior who holds it in their hands.

A Warrior whose eyes fall upon the pages of this tome may improve either their Attack, Strength, Toughness, Weapon Skill, Ballistic Skill, Initiative, or Escape Pinning (RPG: or Luck or Willpower) Characteristic by 1, or they may add 1D6 to their Starting Wounds. This increase is permanent. A Warrior may only read a Book of Metamorphosis once in their lifetime. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
These elegant boots float an inch or so off the ground, their outline shimmering with magical energy.

While wearing these boots a Warrior may levitate a few inches above the ground while moving. While airborne the Warrior may pass over chasms, pits, etc. unhindered and anyone who attacks them suffers -1 To Hit. The boots last for one Adventure, after which they lose their magic and must be discarded. LASTS FOR ONE ADVENTURE
This slender bow was fashioned by the Wood Elves of Loren.

This bow is Strength 4. (RPG: It causes 1D6 Wounds per Battle- level of the wielder). If the Damage is sufficient to kill the target outright, and there is a Monster directly behind it in the direction the bow was fired, the arrow continues. Roll To Hit that Monster too. This process continues until there are no Monsters in the direct line of fire, or one of them survives, or the arrow misses. PERMANENT
This slender, glittering bracelet is said to come from Saphery, created by the Elven Mages who dwelt there in ages past.

When the Warrior finds the bracelet, randomly select one of the Wizard's Starting Spell Cards. This spell is stored in the bracelet, and may be cast once automatically during the Adventure. (RPG: Before each Adventure randomly select one spell with a Casting Number that is equivalent to the Warrior's Battle-level. This spell may be cast automatically.) USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This brooch glows too brightly to look at, as if it contained the energy of a fallen star.

At the end of each Turn, the Wizard may store any unused Power that he has left over within the brooch. This stored Power may then be used at any time to in the same manner as the Wizard's Power Tokens. The brooch can hold a maximum of 6 Power (APG: 6 + Battle-level) at any one time. In addition, the bright aura cast by the brooch distracts any Monsters attacking the Wizard, so they suffer -1 To Hit him. PERMANENT
Every hundred years this bow is passed on to an Elf who performs a great deed.
This Bow is Strength 3 and causes +1 Wound (RPG: +1 Wound per Battle-level). PERMANENT

A Warrior who drinks from this black chalice finds their head filled with visions of war and conflict. A Warrior who drinks from the chalice gets +1 Attack, +1 To Hit and their attacks cause +1D6 Wounds for one Turn. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This dull metal chalice seems wholely unremarkable. Rubbing away the grime of years reveals it to be a Chalice of Sorcery.

This chalice acts as a source of Power that allows the Wizard to carry on casting spells when his own Power has run out. The Wizard can try to draw as many points of Power out of the chalice as he likes. For every point of Power he draws, roll 1D6. All the extra Power must be drawn at once so all the dice are rolled at the same time. For each dice that scores a 1 the Wizard suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. PERMANENT
Lady Chandra de Celeone gave this mirror to the Bretonnian Knight Marius Rand who used it to defeat the Gorgon Thusela in her Black Mountains Lair.

Each time a Monster casts a spell on the Warrior holding this mirror roll 1D6. On a score of 6 the spell is rebounded back onto the caster. (RPG: In addition, any time a Monster with the Petrify ability rolls a natural 1 To Hit the bearer, roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 the Monster's gaze is reflected back on itself and it suffers the full effects of its own gaze attack. If the Monster's Movement ever reaches zero due to this mirror, it turns to stone and is killed.) PERMANENT
A champion wore this helm to defend Estalia during an invasion from Araby.
The wearer of this helm gets +3 Toughness during the first round of each Combat, +2 Toughness during the second round of each Combat and +1 Toughness at all other times. PERMANENT
These cleats have been enchanted by a Wizard from the Colleges of Magic in Altdorf.

When worn by an Ex-Blood Bowl Player, these cleats allow him to change direction once when performing a Running Blitz Attack. They can only be sold at a Foote Locker in a Blood Bowl Stadium. PERMANENT
This dark cloak exudes trails of black smoke, making the wearer undetectable in a dark dungeon.

A Warrior may conceal themselves with this cloak at the star of the Warriors' Phase. They cannot be attacked, but can do nothing else for the rest of the Turn. At the start of the next Turn, the wearer may make a special Ambush Attack against one adjacent Monster. They get +1 To Hit and cause Double Damage. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE

When a Warrior puts this cloak on, a hazy sheen of magical protective power descends upon them. The first time that the wearer of this cloak is hit each Turn, roll 1D6. If the result is a 6, the Warrior is protected by the power of the cloak and the attack, whatever its origin, has no effect. When wearing this cloak, a Warrior cannot wear armour but they may wear a helmet and carry a shield. USE ONCE PER TURN
Michel d'un Doigt (one finger) was a powerful Bretonnian

Wizard. He laboured long to create a series of enchanted artefacts to be used by the chivalric knights of that realm. Unfortunately, the essential ingredient in the creation of each was powdered bone from a human finger. Michel created nine in all... This is the fabled Courage Stone of the Marquis. It is worth 500 gold. (RPG: A Warrior using the Courage Stone may automatically pass one Fear or Terror test.) USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Criscora, a sorceress from Stirland, created this crossbow to defend her residence from a legion of Undead.

Over 100 ghastly creatures fell before she was finally overrun. This Repeating Crossbow is Strength 3, a Range of 8 and can fire up to 2 shots per Turn. It uses no quarrels, firing magical bolts of energy instead. In addition, the first bolt fired in each Turn causes extra Wounds equal to the Power Phase roll ona successful hit. If the Power Phase roll is a 1, the crossbow cannot be fired that Turn. PERMANENT

As soon as they put this crown on, the wearer is plunged into the alien and dark world of magical power familiar to wizards. The wearer takes two Starting Spell Cards of each type at random: these are the spells they may cast just like the Wizard. They may now also use Wizard-only treasure and can still wear Armour.

Each Turn the Warrior gets to cast one or more of their spells just like the Wizard. Each time they cast one of their spells roll1D6. If the score is a 1 that spell fails and the Warrior is immobilised. They can do nothing for the rest of the Turn and any Attacks made against them hit on anything buta 1. PERMANENT
This battered crown is a powerful magic artefact.

A Warrior wearing this crown can detect hostile vibrations in the atmosphere, and is seldom surprised. When Monsters appear who would normally Ambush the Warriors, the wearer should roll 1D6: 1-3. Ambushed as normal. 4-5 The Warrior parries any blows made against them. The Monsters' Ambush Attacks have no effect. 6 As 4-5, and the Warrior gets an extra Attack before the Monsters strike, in addition to any others they may have that Turn. All the other Warriors are ambushed as usual. PERMANENT
This crystal-like stone is mounted in a black iron brooch and radiates a pale light like that of the early morning.
The Dawnstone may be used to restore any one Warrior to full Wounds immediately, even if they are on zero Wounds or died several Turns ago. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Neatly wrapped inside a piece of cloth is a deck of mysterious cards.

This rare deck will fetch 600 gold from the right collector. (RPG: A Warrior may draw one of these cards just before travelling to a Settlement by rolling 1D6. If the score is a 1, the card symbolises misfortune and they must deduct 1 from all rolls made for Travelling Hazards and Settlement Events. If the score is 2, 3, 4 or 5 the card represents protection and they may add +1 to all rolls for Travelling Hazards. If the score is a 6, as per 2-5 above, but the Warrior may also add +1 to all rolls made for Settlement Events.) USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Michel d'un Doigt (one finger) was a powerful Bretonnian

Wizard. He laboured long to create a series of enchanted artefacts to be used by the chivalric knights of that realm. Unfortunately, the essential ingredient in the creation of each was powdered bone from a human finger. Michel created nine in all... This is the fabled Dragon Stone of the Marquis. A Warrior using the Dragon Stone gets +1D6 Toughness for one Turn. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE

This blade is fashioned from the venom fang of a Great Fire Dragon and is ancient beyond ail telling. This sword causes Double Damage on a natural To Hit roll of 5 or 6. However, the sword is partially sentient and refuses to be drawn from its scabbard by unworthy Warriors. At the start of each Turn the wielder wants to use the sword, roll 1D6.
On a roll of 1, 2 or 3 the sword refuses to leave its scabbard. (RPG: It will not let itself be used by Warriors of Battle-level 3 or below.) PERMANENT
This fine weapon is exquisitely carved and possesses a strength far above that of any other bow.
While using this bow a Warrior may make as many shots per Turn as they have Attacks, at their full Strength. PERMANENT
A fine Elf helm. Light but surprisingly strong.

A Warrior wearing this helm gets +1 Toughness. When hit, the wearer may elect to take the blow on the head by rolling 1D6: 1 The Elf Helm absorbs all the Damage from the blow, but is destroyed in the process. 2-4 Resolve the blow as normal. 5-6 The blow glances off, causing no Damage. LASTS UNTIL DESTROYED
This finely crafted shield glitters with an unearthly sheen.
A Warrior using the Elf Shield gets +1 Toughness. In addition, the bearer can ignore the Damage from the first blow they take each Turn unless the attacker's To Hit roll was a natural 6. PERMANENT
This amour looks extremely delicate but is amongst the most effective protection in the known world.
While wearing this armour, a Warrior gets +1 Toughness. In addition, if any of the attacker's Damage Dice score an unmodified 1 or 2, ignore these dice when resolving the Damage. PERMANENT
The surface of this shield gleams as brightly as a mirror. Clouds roll across its surface, and shards of light cut the air around it.
A Warrior using this shield gets +3 Toughness but may not use any other Armour, except for a helmet (RPG: until the wearer is Battle-level 4 or above). PERMANENT
This amulet resembles a stone worn smooth by the action of the sea.

This amulet has the power to heal wounds Each time the wearer uses the amulet roll 1D6: 1 The amulet crumbles to dust and the Warrior suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. 2 The amulet has no effect. 3-6 The Warrior has 1 Wound (RPG: 1 Wound per Battle- level) restored. The amulet fails to work while the Warrior wearing it is on zero Wounds. USE ONCE PER TURN LASTS UNTIL DESTROYED
The gems set into the crown of this wand glow with a flickering light.

Pick a 2 square by 2 square area of the Board Section within the Warrior's Line of Sight. Each Monster in that area suffers 1D6+3 Wounds. When found, the wand has a single charge. (RPG: At the start of each Adventure, the wand will have 1D3 charges. Charges not used by the end of the Adventure are lost.) 1D3 CHARGES PER ADVENTURE

This shard of crystal has a raging fire burning at its core, which flares up to a blazing wall of fire at the wielder's command. Pick a 2 square by 2 square area within the Warrior's Line of Sight. All Monsters in the targeted area suffer 6D6 Wounds Croll once for all the Monsters), with no modifiers for anything.
Brynjar Thundmoor was a Norse warrior renowned for his ability with this flail. It is said that the enchantment in it evolved from his constant battles against wicked foes.
While wielding this flail, a Warrior gets +2 Strength, +1 To Hit and causes +1D6 Wounds. PERMANENT
This pale metallic blue weapon exudes a freezing aura and its blade glitters like ice.

When wielding the Frostblade the Warrior may only make 1 Attack per Turn. If the Warrior makes a successful Attack with the Frostblade that causes at least 1 point of Damage (after all deductions and Special Abilities), the target is automatically slain. A blow from a Frostblade cannot cause a Death-blow. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
These cold steel gauntlets have been steeped in powerful magic, and are able to tear the life essence from their victim.

With these gauntlets, a Warrior may make an extra Attack every Turn, which must be made before the Warrior makes any other Attacks. The Attack with the Gauntlets suffers -1 To Hit and causes normal Damage. (RPG: The first time that the Warrior kills a Monster with these gauntlets in every Adventure, roll 1D6. On a score of 4, 5 or 6 they add +1 Wound to their Starting Wounds score permanently.) PERMANENT

These small gems sparkle with a strange, otherworldly energy. This pair of gems is worth 700 gold. (RPG: One of these gems should be worn while the other is kept in a safe place away from the Dungeon. If the wearer is killed, shortly after their death their body starts to dwindle in size until it is a pinprick of light contained with the gem. The only things left behind are mundane items such as bandages, the Lantern or a rope.

If the other Warriors survive the rest of the Dungeon and bring the two gems back together, their magical power recalls the dead Warrior, alive and well, just as they were before the Adventure started.) USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
A magical, freezing sheet of ice envelops a portion of this axe.

Each time a Warrior wounds a target with this axe, roll 1D6. If the score is equal to or greater than the target's Toughness, it suffers an additional 1D3 Wounds and is encased in a sarcophagus of ice and may not move or fight. While entombed, the target cannot be attacked. Roll 1D6 at the beginning of the next Monsters' Phase. If the score is greater than the target's Toughness, the target suffers another 1D3 Wounds and remains encased. If the score is less than the target's Toughness, then the ice melts and the target is free. PERMANENT
A metal skull acts as the clasp for this studded belt.
While wearing this belt a Warrior gets +1 Strength. If worn by a Pit Fighter, he also gets +1 Attack. PERMANENT
Golden runes gleam on this blade, granting the wielder the first chance to strike.

While wielding this sword, a Warrior will always go first in the Warriors' Phase unless another rule specifies otherwise. In addition, the wielder may add +2 to their roll to Escape from Pinning. PERMANENT
This sword feels as light as wood but is more powerful than regular steel.
While a Warrior is using this word they get +1 To Hit against any Undead creature. In addition, once per Adventure it causes +3D6 Wounds to one Undead creature. PERMANENT USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The liquid in this bottle is thin like water and has a floral aroma.

A Warrior who drinks this potion has 1D6 Wounds restored. (RPG: In addition, a Warrior who drink this antidote will restore all the points to their Characteristics that had previously been reduced for any reason. This potion cannot restore Wounds that have been permanently reduced.) USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This mighty axe is a Dwarf artefact, able to cut through all but the most powerful armour.

This two-handed axe ignores non-Magical Armour and causes Double Damage. However, the axe is partially sentient and refuses to be drawn by unworthy Warriors. At the start of each Turn that the bearer wishes to use it, roll 1D6. On a roll of 1 or 2 the axe cannot be drawn. (APG: It will not let itself be used by Warriors of Battle-level 2 or below.) PERMANENT
This halberd was created by the High Elves of Ulthuan to defend against the constant raids from Naggaroth.
When wielding this two-handed weapon in Combat a Warrior gets +1 Strength, +3 Toughness and may attack from up to 2 squares away in hand-to-hand Combat. PERMANENT
An aura of deep hatred surrounds this wicked halberd.

A Warrior using this two-handed weapon gets +2 Strength and may attack from up to 2 squares away in hand-to-hand Combat. If used against Orcs, the wielder gets double their regular Attacks but is so full of rage that they suffer -1 To Hit. PERMANENT
A magical rune of an anvil has been emblazoned upon this hammer.

The Hammer ignores non-magical Armour. In addition, if the To Hit roll is a 6, the target's Armour is destroyed! If the target's Armour is magical, however, roll another 1D6. On a 6 the magical Armour is destroyed. PERMANENT
This weapon was given to the legendary hero Sigmar by King

Kurgan Ironbeard, to cement an alliance between Dwarfs and Men. It is perhaps the most ancient and revered of all the magic weapons in the Empire. The Hammer of Sigmar ignores all except Magical Armour when rolling for Damage. In addition, if the To Hit roll was a natural 6, it causes Quadruple Damage. There can only ever be one Hammer of Sigmar in the party. PERMANENT
This Hammer seeks certain Monsters, to be slain at all costs.

Choose a Monster as it is placed on the Board as the hammer's hated foe. If there is an empty space adjacent to that Monster, the wielder of the hammer may be placed in that space, no matter how far away or what Monsters intervene. The Warrior gets an immediate Ambush Attack against that Monster, and gains +1 Attack every Turn against that Monster until it is dead. If the wielder kills the Monster, they gain double its Gold Value. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This two-handed weapon has been suffused with the skin shed by a Coatl, a mystical creature from the land of Lustria.

While wielding the Hell Bird, in addition to their regular Attacks, a Warrior may leap into the air to make an extra Death Chop Attack into an adjacent square. Roll To Hit as normal. If the Attack hits, then the model in that square, as well as any model in the square directly behind it suffers Damage as normal Croll separately for each modelO> The Death Chop may not cause a Death-blow. USE ONCE PER TURN
Flames leap and burn along the length of this other-worldly sword. As it swings through the air it leaves a trail of spitting magma in its wake.

If the wielder hits their opponent with a natural To Hit roll of 6, as well as causing normal Damage the target and all adjacent models to the target (including the wielder!) burst into flames and take an extra 1D6+1 (RPG: 1D6 + Battle-level) Wounds, with no modifiers for Toughness or Armour. PERMANENT
This glittering helmet was worn by Alcadizzar as he rode into battle against the Undead hordes.

It is said that the helm had the magic ability to store the light of the sun, and the Undead would flee in terror from the blazing figure of Alcadizzar as he galloped across the battlefield. This helm illuminates the area allowing the wearer to explore just as if they carried the Lantern. In addition, they get +1 Toughness, and any Undead attacking them suffer -1 To Hit. PERMANENT
The wings fashioned on this helm have been deliberately sharpened.
While wearing this helmet a Warrior gets +1 Toughness. In addition, if worn by an Ex-Blood Bowl Player, he causes an extra +1D3 Wounds on each successful hit during a Running Blitz Attack. PERMANENT
This black helm has no apparent visor, yet it imparts upon the wearer the ability to see clearly even in the dim light of a dungeon.
A Warrior who wears this helm gets +1 Toughness. In addition, this helmet allows the wearer to see in the dark and explore just as if they carried the Lantern. PERMANENT
From within the depths of this ugly helm, the Warrior's eyes stare out with an otherworldly intensity.

A Warrior wearing this helm gets +1 Toughness. In addition, once per Turn, the Warrior wearing the helm may attempt to hypnotise any one Monster that is within their Line of Sight. Roll 1D6. On a score of 6, the Monster is hypnotised for one Turn. Whilst hypnotised, the Monster loses 1D6 Attacks. PERMANENT USE ONCE PER TURN
Michel d'un Doigt (one finger) was a powerful Bretonnian

Wizard. He laboured long to create a series of enchanted artefacts to be used by the chivalric knights of that realm. Unfortunately, the essential ingredient in the creation of each was powdered bone from a human finger. Michel created nine in all... This is the fabled Hero Stone of the Marquis. A Warrior using the Hero Stone gets +1D3 Attacks for one Turn. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
These wooden tools were cut from the branches of a sacred tree, hardened in a holy flame and blessed by a priest of Sigmar.

Once per Adventure, a Warrior adjacent to a Vampire in Combat may attempt to use the stake to slay the creature. Roll 1D6. If the score is a natural 6, the Warrior manages to strike the creature directly in the heart and it dies immediately. This roll cannot be affected by Luck, Faith or any other means. DISCARD AFTER SUCCESSFUL USE (From 'Vampire Slayers' Adventure)
This bronze horn shines as if it was recently polished.

When blown, the magical power of this horn allows all the Warriors to parry incoming blows. When a Warrior is attacked in hand-to-hand Combat, roll 1D6. On a score of 6 they turn the blow and it causes no Damage (RPG: They gain the Parry 6+ ability). The effect lasts until the Combat ends, or a 1 is rolled in the Power Phase. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
As this horn is blown a howling wind tosses the Monsters to the ground.

When a Warrior blows this horn. roll 1D6 for every Monster on the same Board Section. If the score is more than the Monster's Toughness, it is hurled to the ground and suffers 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for Armour. It will take a Monster a whole Turn to get up. During this time it may do nothing else. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Steeped in the blood of a hydra as it was forged, this blade has taken on some of the properties of these terrible beasts.
For one Attack, this blade causes +6D6 Wounds. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This lantern is more than a light source...

When activated, this magical lantern can be used just like the leader's Lantern. In addition, the bearer can never be ambushed by Monsters. At the end of each Combat, the bearer may roll 1D6. On a score of 6, they receive an extra Dungeon Room Treasure which does not count toward their share. (RPG: The bearer may also add +1 to any rolls to find secret doors). The lantern draws power from whoever carries it. If the Power Phase roll is a 1, the bearer must roll 1D6. If the result is a 1 or 2, the bearer must subtract 1 from their Starting Wounds score permanently. The lantern cannot be turned off until the bearer exits the Dungeon. PERMANENT
This thick iron gauntlet is covered with studs in the shape of Dwarf runes.

A Warrior who wears the Left Gauntlet of Damzhar gets +1 Strength, as well as +1 Attack with the weapon they hold in the gauntlet. If a Warrior wears both Gauntlets of Damzhar, they get a total of +2 Strength and +1 Attack for each weapon they wield (or +2 Attacks if holding a two-handed weapon). In addition, once per Adventure, they gain a special Gauntlet Attack that gets +1 To Hit and causes 4D6 Damage (do not add Strength to the score). PERMANENT
Michel d'un Doigt Cone finger) was a powerful Bretonnian

Wizard. He laboured long to create a series of enchanted artefacts to be used by the chivalric knights of that realm. Unfortunately, the essential ingredient in the creation of each was powdered bone from a human finger. Michel created nine in all... This is the fabled Life Stone of the Marquis. The bearer can use this magical stone at any time to ignore the Damage from a single blow that would otherwise kill them. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Ashraaz was one of the insane sorcerers of Araby. Fables tell that he created mystical stones which sucked the essence of life from the air itself.
Each Turn the bearer may use the Lifestone to restore 1D3 Wounds, so long as the Warrior is on 1 or more Wounds when they choose to use it. USE ONCE PER TURN
Bolts of energy streak up and down the blade of this two- handed spear.

A Warrior may throw this spear as a missile weapon (Range 4, may be retrieved at the end of Combat) or may use it to attack from up to 2 squares away in hand-to-hand Combat. If the To Hit roll is a 6, then the target suffers an additional 2D6 Lightning Damage (no modifiers for Armour). Roll another 1D6. If the score is a 5 or 6, the bolt arcs to the next nearest Monster and hits it for 2D6 Wounds (no modifiers for Armour). This process continues until the bolt fails to arc. A Monster may not be hit a subsequent time. PERMANENT
Michel d'un Doigt (one finger) was a powerful Wizard from

Bretonnian. He laboured long to create a series of enchanted artefacts to be used by the chivalric knights of that realm. Unfortunately, the essential ingredient in the creation of each was powdered bone from a human finger. Michel created nine in all... This is the fabled Mending Stone of the Marquis. A Warrior using the Mending Stone is restored to full Wounds. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Carvings of tormented souls line the frame of this mirror.

Once per Combat a Warrior may show this mirror to an adjacent Monster by rolling 1D6. If the score is equal to or greater than the Monster's Toughness, it becomes trapped inside the mirror and is removed from the Board. At the start of each Turn thereafter, roll 1D6. If the score is a 1 or 2, the Monster escapes and is placed next to the Warrior with the mirror (moving models as necessary). It receives an immediate attack against the Warrior, and then attacks as normal in the Monsters' Phase. The bearer can shatter the mirror at any time whereupon any Monster inside is killed. The Warriors do not receive any gold for a Monster killed in this manner. USE ONCE PER COMBAT LASTS UNTIL SHATTERED
This magic-imbued and restrain even the strongest creatures!

A Warrior may throw this net at a single Monster up to 2 squares away by rolling 1D6 (a Pit Fighter may add +1 to the roll). If the score is a 4, 5 or 6, the target is netted for the rest of the Combat and suffers -1 To Hit. Any Warrior attacking a netted Monster gets +1 To Hit it. The net may be retrieved at the end of the Combat. PERMANENT
Strange images flash inside this crystal and the Warriors swear that they caught a glimpse of themselves.

A Warrior can use this orb to see what is beyond each Doorway on the current Board Section. This includes drawing Dungeon or Event Cards and rolling on any tables or sub-tables as a result. If the Warriors choose to enter those new Board Sections, they must roll 1D6. If the score is a 1, the orb was wrong and the Warriors must interact with the Board Section anew. If the score is 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6, then the orb was accurate and they must play it as it was foretold. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
Nestled within an engraved wooden box, this pistol looks as expensive as it does deadly.

The pistol is a missile weapon with Strength 6 and a Range of 6. It takes one Turn to reload after it has been fired. The pistol comes with enough gunpowder and shot to last for for one Adventure. PERMANENT
Michel d'un Doigt (one finger) was a powerful Wizard from

Bretonnian. He laboured long to create a series of enchanted artefacts to be used by the chivalric knights of that realm. Unfortunately, the essential ingredient in the creation of each was powdered bone from a human finger. Michel created nine in all... This is the fabled Power Stone of the Marquis. A Warrior using the Power Stone gets +1D6 to their Strength for one Turn. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This curious talisman brings rare bouts of good fortune to its wearer.

This talisman is worth 500 gold. (RPG: The power of this talisman can be invoked during a single stay at a Settlement. It allows the bearer to re-roll one Settllement Event per day, but they must accept the second result, even if it's worse. PERMANENT
The tips of these vicious quarrels are designed to cause as much injury as possible.

A Warrior using these quarrels gets +1 To Hit. In addition, they cause Double Damage if the To Hit roll is a natural 6. There are 2D6 quarrels. Each quarrel may be used only once. DISCARD WHEN ALL USED
The serrated points along the cutting edge of this blade grind and gnash together like teeth, tearing through armour, flesh and bone.
This sword causes +2D6 Wounds. In addition, if the To Hit roll was a natural 5 or 6, the blow ignores 2 points of Armour. PERMANENT
Sprinkling a mixture of earth and water, the Warrior bearing the scroll casts a magical enchantment that reverts stone back to flesh.

This spell may be cast on a single Warrior who has been turned to stone. The Warrior returns to normal but must roll 1D6. If the result is a 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6, then the Warrior suffers no lasting effects from the petrification. If the score is a 1, however, then the Warrior must deduct 1D3 from their Starting Wounds _ score permanently. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This thick iron gauntlet is covered with studs in the shape of Dwarf runes.

A Warrior who wears the Right Gauntlet of Damzhar gets +1 Strength, as well as +1 Attack with the weapon they hold in the gauntlet. If a Warrior wears both Gauntlets of Damzhar, they get a total of +2 Strength and +1 Attack for each weapon they wield (or +2 Attacks if holding a two-handed weapon). In addition, once per Adventure, they gain a special Gauntlet Attack that gets +1 To Hit and causes 4D6 Damage (do not add Strength to the score). PERMANENT
Cheshnakk was a great wizard from Araby whose expertise was the creation of exquisite flying carpets.

The pinnacle of his achievement, however, was forging the Rings of Cheshnakk. On command, one of these rings can transport the bearer to safety. This exquisite and mysterious ring is worth 1000 gold. (RPG: While wearing this ring a Warrior may at any time leave the Dungeon and be transported to safety. They are out of this Adventure and meet the other Warriors at the next Settlement.) USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This ring has two crystal hands clutching a gold nugget that emits a soft, warm glow.

While a Warrior wears this ring, they may ignore the effects of any diseases they carry. (RPG: In addition, if the rules ever state that the bearer is affected by Paralysis, Petrify, Poison, or Fatal Damage, roll 1D6. If the score is a 5 or 6, the bearer is immune to the effects.) PERMANENT
This golden ring is said to be imbued with the power of Orion, the King of the Woods.

The bearer of this ring gets +2 Strength. (RPG: If the bearer must ever subtract a point permanently from one of their Characteristics (due to Poison for example), it may be deducted from the ring instead. The ring now only gives the bearer +1 Strength and is worth only 250 gold. If the ring is used a second time, it is drained and becomes useless and worthless.) DISCARD WHEN DRAINED
This stone band holds the power of life itself, healing even the most grievous of wounds.

At the end of any Turn in which the wearer of this ring has suffered Wounds, they may attempt to heal by regenerating with the ring. To do this, roll 1D6. If they are currently above zero Wounds, then on a score of 6 they have 2D6 Wounds restored. If they are on zero Wounds, then on a score of 6 they have 1D6 Wounds restored. USE ONCE PER TURN
This glittering ring is fashioned from a material that looks like quicksilver.

When worn, the ring constantly shifts and changes like a shining, liquid-metal band. This ring allows the Warrior to attempt to avoid a trap they have just activated. Roll 1D6. On a score of 4, 5 or 6 the trap is magically disarmed and fails to work. PERMANENT

This black ring constantly moves from finger to finger, blinking and shimmering with an unholy light. Instead of moving normally, the wearer may use the ring to teleport. They must declare that they are using the ring before they move. Roll 1D6. The result shows how many squares they may move by teleporting. The Warrior must move to an empty square. If there is no empty square, the Warrior must stay where they are and can do nothing for the rest of the Turn.
When teleporting, the Warrior ignores any obstacles and automatically Escapes from Pinning. USE ONCE PER TURN
Rockjaw Sevenfingers was famous for eating stonebread and throwing axes. It is said that after he had this magical axe created, it took him three unfortunate attempts to master it.

A Warrior wielding this axe gets +1 Strength. It can be used in hand-to-hand Combat or as a missile weapon. If thrown as a missile weapon the axe will return to the Warrior. After resolving the attack, roll 1D6. On a score of 3, 4, 5 or 6 the Warrior catches the axe safely. On a 1 or 2 the Warrior cuts themselves while catching the axe and suffer 1D6 Wounds with no modifiers for anything. PERMANENT
This silver chalice is covered in tear-dropped shaped rubies.

A Warrior may drink from this chalice any time during Combat. If they then suffer Wounds from a Monster for any reason, the Warrior immediately gets +1 Toughness. If they suffer Wounds again, the Warrior gets an additional +1 Toughness, and so on up to a maximum of +3 Toughness. The effects of the chalice last until the end of the Combat or until the Warrior is reduced to zero Wounds. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This stone has a Dwarf rune embossed upon its surface and is warm to the touch.

When pressed into contact with any bladed weapon, the runestone burns a Rune of Annihilation into the blade and then vanishes. The Rune of Annihilation itself is permanent. If the To Hit roll with this weapon is a natural 6, the weapon causes +2D6 Wounds. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This stone has a Dwarf rune embossed upon its surface and is warm to the touch.

When pressed into contact with any bladed weapon, the runestone burns a Rune of Restoration into the blade and then vanishes. The Rune of Restoration itself is permanent. A Warrior who wields this weapon automatically has 1 Wound restored at the end of each Turn. It fails to work while the bearer is on zero Wounds. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
The Sceptre of Zandri protects its wielder from the baneful gaze of the Undead, making them harder to hit.
The Sceptre may be wielded like a weapon, and causes +1D6 Wounds against any Undead. In addition, any Undead attacking the wielder suffer -2 To Hit. PERMANENT
This shield throws a shimmering haze around its bearer, making them a difficult target in battle.

A Warrior using this shield gets +2 Toughness. In addition, when drawing Warrior Counters to determine who has been hit by missile fire, the first time the bearer's counter is pulled out they may put it back and draw again. If it comes up a second time they are hit as normal. PERMANENT
Shouting words of power, the Warrior bearing the scroll is surrounded with a glowing screen of magical energy.
When used, the Warrior bearing the scroll is immune to all attacks except those made with either a natural 6 To Hit or from spells (or similar magic) for one Turn. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Michel d'un Doigt (one finger) was a powerful Bretonnian

Wizard. He laboured long to create a series of enchanted artefacts to be used by the chivalric knights of that realm. Unfortunately, the essential ingredient in the creation of each was powdered bone from a human finger. Michel created nine in all... This is the fabled Shield Stone of the Marquis. A Warrior using the Shield Stone may ignore the effects of any one spell cast against them. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
The Warrior hurls a spear of pure energy at their chosen victim.
When used, any single Monster in the bearer's Line of Sight immediately suffers 4D6 Wounds. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
Michel d'un Doigt (one finger) was a powerful Bretonnian

Wizard. He laboured long to create a series of enchanted artefacts to be used by the chivalric knights of that realm. Unfortunately, the essential ingredient in the creation of each Was powdered bone from a human finger. Michel created nine in all... This is the fabled Speed Stone of the Marquis. A Warrior using the Speed Stone gets +2 Movement and may automatically Escape from Pinning for one Turn. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This rune contains powerful spells of negation. Magic in the immediate vicinity arcs as the rune tries to pull it in and absorb it.

When pressed into contact with any bladed weapon, the Spelleater Rune burns a copy of itself into the blade and then vanishes. The rune on the blade itself is permanent. Each time a spell is cast against the bearer of this weapon, roll 1D6. If the score is a 5 or 6, the spell has no effect on them (RPG: the wielder gets the Magic Resistance 5+ ability). USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This robe shimmers with an eerie dark purple glow.

A Warrior wearing this robe becomes ethereal upon command. They may automatically Escape from Pinning and may move freely through other models and obstacles. Any attacks made with a non-magical weapon against the wearer suffer -1 To Hit. The effects last for one Turn. The Spirit Robe may not be worn with any helmet or armour. USE ONCE PER TURN
This staff had runes of command burned into it many centuries ago.

Once per Event the Wizard may use this staff to attempt to control any Monster on the Board. The attempt must be made as the Monsters appear. Roll 1D6. On a score of 5 or 6 the Wizard may choose one Monster and make it do whatever he wants for one Turn as long as the action does not cause it to harm itself. The Monster fights in the Warriors' Phase and if it kills another Monster, no one gets the gold. USE ONCE PER EVENT
This staff was created by a mage to protect his village against the repeated attacks from Chaos Dwarfs.

Any Chaos Dwarf Sorcerer who attempts to cast a spell while on the same Board Section as the wielder of this staff must roll 1D6. If the result is a 5 or 6, the spell has no effect. The staff can be used as a weapon, causing Damage as normal. If used against a Chaos Dwarf, the wielder gets +1 To Hit and causes +1D6 Wounds. PERMANENT
The thin purple liquid in this vial can promote a strong vitality.

A Warrior who drinks this liquid may add +1 to their Starting Wounds score permanently. If an Ex-Blood Bowl Player drinks it, he may add +1D3 to his Starting Wounds score permanently. USE ONCE THEN DISCARD
This elegant bow has a single Rune of Surefire carved on it.
Ancient stories tell of a bow that never misses and this may be that very weapon... This bow is Strength 4. A Warrior using this bow gets +2 To Hit. PERMANENT
A dying High Elf Mage imbued this sword with a desire for vengeance against his killers.
This sword causes Double Damage against Dark Elves (Triple Damage if the To Hit roll is a natural 6). Against any other opponent it causes only +1 Wound. PERMANENT
This sword resonates with a deep hum and flickers with lightning.

When drawn from its scabbard, this sword nullifies all magic within 1 square of the wielder. As long as the sword remains drawn, the wielder and any models in adjacent squares cannot use or benefit from any spells or other magic items. In addition, the sword causes +1 Wound (APG: and gives the bearer the Magic Dispel 6+ ability). PERMANENT
This sword shines with the undimming fire of righteousness - smiting down evil wherever it may be found.
This sword causes an extra +3D6 Wounds when used against Monsters with a Toughness of 6 or more. PERMANENT
This sword was forged in Hoeth, in the realm of the High Elves. It is lightning fast and, once mastered, makes the wielder all but unstoppable in battle.

When using this sword a Warrior must roll 1D6 each Turn. On a score of 5 or 6 they gain double their regular Attacks. On a score of 2, 3 or 4 they attack as normal. On a score of 1 the Warrior trips and falls over and may do nothing at all for the rest of the Turn. PERMANENT
This blade shines with a pure light and never dulls.

Attacks made with this sword ignore Toughness and non- Magical Armour when determining Damage. In addition, once per Turn the wielder can re-roll one Attack with the sword that missed. PERMANENT USE ONCE PER TURN

This stone table weighs surprisingly little and is covered in indecipherable hatchings and markings. This magical tablet is worth 1000 gold. (RPG: When used by a Wizard, the tablet allows them to re-roll any or all of his 'spell determining' dice each time he goes up a Battle-level (see New Spells in the Wizards' Training section).
They may only re-roll each dice once and must take the result of the second roll, even if it is worse.) PERMANENT
This glittering black jewel feels warm to the touch. This talisman allows a Wizard to exchange one of the spells he has learned for a new one.

If he wants to exchange one of his spells, roll 1D6. On a score of 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6 he may exchange the spell he doesn't want for another of the same type. Select the new spell at random. (RPG: If he wishes to exchange one of his spells for another, roll 1D6. On a score of 1 the talisman crumbles to dust and must be discarded. On a score of 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6 the Wizard may exchange one of his spells for one of the same Casting Number.) USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE
This black talisman throbs dully with the rhythm of a pulsing wound. This talisman interferes with the powers of nearby spellcasters.

Any spells cast by models adjacent to the bearer of the talisman fail on a 1D6 roll of 4, 5 or 6 and the Power used to cast the spell is redirected to restore 1D6 of the wearer's Wounds. If the wearer attempts to cast a spell, roll 1D6. On a score of 1 the spell fails. PERMANENT
This immense blade was created by the High Elves of Ulthuan to defend against the constant raids from Naggaroth.
Attacks made with this two-handed sword suffer -1 To Hit. In addition, roll 1D6 at the beginning of each Turn to see what other benefit it bestows: 1-4 +3 Toughness. 5-6 +4 Toughness. PERMANENT
This wand is pure white, shot through with streaks of diabolum, a bright red substance capable of harnessing magical energy.
While using this wand, all of the Wizard's spells cost one less Power to cast. PERMANENT

This wand has strange icons engraved upon its surface. This wand increases the effect of any spell that involves rolling dice for its effects by adding +2 to the total. The Wand does not increase the chance of success, however. For example, if used to increase the effects of the Finger of Life spell, the spell still succeeds on a roll of 4, 5 or 6, but now heals 6, 7 or 8 Wounds (depending on the success roll).
When found, the wand has 2D6 charges. Each use of the wand drains 1 charge. DISCARD WHEN DRAINED

A soft hum emanates from this mysterious wand. This wand contains spells that a Wizard may cast. Take 1D6 Starting Spell Cards not currently in play to represent the spells contained in the wand. The bearer may cast each spell once automatically at no Power Cost. (RPG: Once all the spells are used, the wand may be recharged at the Font of Power for 1D6x100 gold.
Follow the rules above to see which spells are now stored in the wand.) USE THEN RECHARGE (From 'Rooftop Murder' Adventure) OM TREASURE

This deceptively crude-looking spear can penetrate even the most robust armour. This spear may be used in hand-to-hand Combat or as a missile weapon up to 6 squares away. It ignores up to 2 points of non- magical Armour (1 point if the Armour is magical).
If thrown, the spear also causes Double Damage if the To Hit roll is a 5 or 6. If may be retrieved at the end of the Combat. PERMANENT
This sky-blue jewel is said to be linked to the god Asuryan himself and imparts lightning-fast reactions to the wearer.

When ambushed, the bearer rolls 1D6. On a score of 6, they sense the danger and warn the other Warriors, so the attackers do not get their Ambush Attack. The Warrior may try this once per Event which generates Monsters with the Ambush ability. USE ONCE PER EVENT

When they use this arcane jewel, the Warriors vanish with a thundercrack. The jewel bearer and all adjacent Warriors are transported to anywhere in the Dungeon already explored, appearing in the same order as when they disappeared. If a Warrior who is transported is carrying the only light source, the remaining Warriors are Lost in the Dark and must roll on the Escaping the Dungeon Table. This also happens if Warriors transport without a light source. USE ONCE PER ADVENTURE

Your Warrior's wild behaviour attracts the attention of the Settlement authorities and there are thrown outoftown.They must wait outside the Settlement gates for the other Warriors. In addition,roll1D6.On a score of1 your Warrior is fleeced by the gate guards asthey are thrown outand have alltheir gold stolen!
See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

Your Warrior is invited by a group of locals togo on a nocturnal hunt. Meeting them just before sunset,they are informed that tonight's prey is the great Qu arg a beast of fearsome temper andfouldisposition.Your Warrior's task in the group is to take the hunting tools- a net,a sm all pole with a bellon it and a bag full of garlic and wait in the middle of the woods while the rest of the hunters spread out into the forest and drive theQuarg towardsthem.Asthesunrisesthenextmorning,and theowls return to their nests, there is stillno sign of the Quarg,your Warrior begins to wonder if someone is being madeafoolof...

Your Warrior is accused of witchcraft,andis chased through the streets by an angry mob. Roll1D6 on the following table (the Witch Hunter may add +1tohisscore): 1-3 Your Warrior escapes by jumping over the Settlement wall and into the filthy water of the moat.Pelted with eggsandrottenfruit,theystaggeroutonto thefar rules (page 3). 4-5 Your Warrior ducks down aside alley andescapes,but from now on must wear a disguise when out in public! 6 Your Warrior turns angrily on the mob,demandingan explanation.Shoutingdowntheleaderof the m ob,they establish theirauthorityoverthecrowd,who sheepishly returnto theirhovels,pausingonlytogive your Warrior 1o0goldas way of compensation.

As your Warrior retires for the evening they fall into a deep slumber.Theybeginto dream that they are fighting a terrifying gorgon;herhaircomposedofvicioussnakesand agaze which can turn a person to stone. with a horrendous scream the gorgon attacks! Roll 1D6 to see how the dream ends: 1 No matter how many times or how hardyour Warrior strikes the creature with their weapon,the beast seemsinvulnerable.Withaterrifying scream the gorgon shootsan arrow directly into your Warrior's heart! Whenyour Warriorwakes,theyfindthat theyhave permanently lost 1 Wound!Ifyour Warrior's Starting Woundsiseverreducedtozerobecauseofthisdream, theyaredead. Tomorrow night the nightmare returns and they must roll on this table again.MakeanoteonyourAdventure Record Sheet to remind you. 2-5 The battle is brutal and neither your Warrior nor the creatureseemstogettheupperhand.YourWarrior wakesin acold sweat. Tomorrow night the nightmare returns and they must roll on this table again!MakeanoteonyourAdventure Record Sheet to remind you. Your Warrior courageously stands their ground and 6 strikes the wretchedbeastdown!A sense of relief fills your Warrior and they sleep better than they have in a long time. Whenyour Warrior awakens they discover that they are +1to his Starting Wounds!The nightmare ends and theydonothavetorollonthistableagain(unless theyencounterthisSettlementEventagain)! If your Warrior leaves the Settlement before the dream concludes, the nightmare temporarily ends. At the next Settlement, however,it returns to haunt the Warrior again. MakeanoteonyourAdventureRecordSheet.

Strolling alongside a dilapidated oldhouse your Warrior spies an old,stone well.Your Warrior casually tosses in 1 gold coin.Roll 1D6 on the table below: 1 Acreepy,gutturalmoanechoesupwardinanunknown tongue.A terrible sense of dread overwhelms your Warriorand theysuffer-1LuckforthenextAdventure. 2-5 Nothing happens and your Warrior is now carrying one less gold piece. 6 A high pitched squeal and soft giggling can be heard at the bottom of the well.Your Warrior gains +1Luck for next Adventure!

Asyour Warriors trolls through the village centre it is abuzz with thepreparationsfortomorrow'sfestival.Roll1D6tosee what festival is being celebrated and another 1D6tosee what effect ithason ALL ofyour Warriors. 1D6 Festival A Beer festival 1 2 A Food festival A festival for the Deceased 3 4 A Religious festival A Music festival 5 6 A Cultural festival 1D6 Effect 1 In recognition of thefestival,alllocations willbe closed forthenext1D6 days.Warriorswhoaretrainingwill find their instruction interrupted during the period and they mustadd on those extra days to their training time. 2 In recognition of the festival, all traders will close up shopforthenext1D3days. 3 The population of the Village will double as it welcomes visitors from thesurroundingregion.Forthenext1D3 days all Warriors must roll on the Town Events Table instead of the Village Events Table.In addition, Warriors may make all stock rolls as if in a Town (2D6). 4 Vendors,merchantsandtraders will make their wayto the Village tomorrow and any Warrior may visitthe Peddler, who is treated exactly asthe Peddler from the Role play Book (page 15)orthenew,expanded Travelling Hazards by Little monk (page 8).Visitingthe Peddler takes an entire day during which a Warrior 5 In celebration (but mostly to lure in new customers)all locations will offer one item in stock at5o%.Thissale is offered tomorrow only. 6 In celebration (but m ostly tolurein customers)all locations willoffer1o% of fall items in stock.Thissale is offered tomorrow only! If any Warrior rolls this Settlement Event again during this stay,re-rollthis result.

An item of Treasure that your Warrior is carrying (determined randomly)isidentifiedbya wealthy,influentialmemberof the local citizenry ashisfamilyheirloom,stolenlastyearby wanton burglars.The local Watch confiscates theitem and you must discarditimmediately.If your Warrior has no Treasure items, the wealthy local accuses your Warrior of hiding it and demands 1D6x50 goldin restitutioninstead.Ifthey cannotpay,then your
